ArchitectureNotes.objective: MINT replicates Claude's document generation pipeline as a standalone, model-agnostic toolkit. Plan optimizes for G0→G1→G2→G5→G6 minimum viable launch path. Phase 4 adds modular generation: decompose document into sections, generate each independently, assemble deterministically.
ArchitectureNotes.non-goal: No Claude API, no dvejsada dependency, no hosted SaaS, no macros/signatures/protection in v0.x.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-1: Frontier models may fail to produce valid docx-js/pptxgenjs code — G0 kill criterion. Mitigated by modular generation (Phase 4): smaller scope per call = higher success rate.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-2: Code execution sandbox security — restricted environment with no fs/net/timeout.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-3: Template engine must handle 7B model JSON reliably or G0 fails.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-4: Plan quality depends on model's ability to decompose user prompt into structured sections. Mitigated by plan validation + retry with plan refinement prompt.
ArchitectureNotes.architecture-pattern-1: M-CREATE → skill → model → sandbox → validate. Works for Claude-class models. 33% success on 35B.
ArchitectureNotes.architecture-pattern-2: M-PLAN → M-SECTION[] → M-ASSEMBLE → validate. Each section is small enough for 35B. Plan is cheap JSON. Assembly is deterministic. ~90% success rate expected.
ArchitectureNotes.architecture-pattern-3: M-EDIT receives a typed JSON EditPlan, not raw OOXML. M-OOXML unpacks/repacks the container; M-EDIT applies typed operations over the lxml tree. Tracked changes and comments follow ECMA-376; reply linkage lives in word/commentsExtended.xml.
ArchitectureNotes.architecture-pattern-4: From v0.3 MINT runs under one of two execution engines, selected by `MINT_ENGINE=python|js` (default: python). The chokepoint is M-CLI._select_engine; the surface is the `engine` field on M-CONFIG.MintConfig. Migration scope is asymmetric: M-VALIDATE / M-RULES / M-FIX / M-FINGERPRINT / M-OOXML / M-EDIT / M-GRACE / M-EXTRACT / M-PLAN are ALREADY pure Python and are unaffected by the dual-runtime split. The legacy Node.js dependency exists only inside M-SANDBOX (vm-based JS execution) and the modules that route through it (M-CREATE single-pass, M-ASSEMBLE, M-SECTION). Phase-6 (Phase 0 of the pure-python rollout) ships only the dispatch chokepoint + the empty MP-PKG skeleton; later phases populate `mint_python.execution.{small,medium,frontier}` to replace M-SANDBOX, and `mint_python.sdk` to replace docx-js/pptxgenjs as the model-targeted authoring surface. Pure Python is the strategic default; the JS engine is an opt-in fallback retained for the existing fixture corpus and for the docx-js model-output path until parity is reached. **Living spec** for the migration is split between `docs/pure-python/` (per-aspect notes: handover-v1, structure, feature-flag, acceptance-criteria) and the consolidated `docs/mint-pure-python-handover-v1.md` (§5 module structure, §6 phase plan, §7 acceptance criteria). Agents reading this plan MUST consult both before designing any MP-* module contract. **Deprecation horizon for M-SANDBOX (phase-gated, not calendar-gated).** Fully-live legacy until BOTH conditions hold: (a) handover rollout Phase 4 (Execution tiers) ships — operationalized as **`MP-EXEC-FRONTIER` (and the small/medium siblings) registered in `docs/knowledge-graph.xml` with `STATUS="done"`**, providing a concrete boolean a swarm worker / controller can grep for; AND (b) VF-012 PurePythonRegression is green on the showcase corpus. When both gates are met, M-SANDBOX flips to `STATUS="deprecated"` and a one-line deprecation warning fires on `MINT_ENGINE=js` resolution at startup. Removal happens one minor version after deprecation lands (e.g. if deprecation = v0.X, removal = v0.X+1). Version numbers are intentionally NOT pinned here — the previous v0.4/v0.5 wording was calendar fiction because rollout Phase 4 (per handover §6) cannot land in v0.4 alongside Phase 1. If parity work slips, deprecation slips with it.

terminology.term-1: "Phase-6" — internal dev-plan ImplementationOrder slot, follows historical Phase-1..Phase-5. Refers to Phase 0 (skeleton + flag) of the pure-python rollout. Not the same as "Phase 0".
terminology.term-2: "Phase 0/1/2/3/4/5" (per handover §6) — pure-python rollout phases. Phase 0 = skeleton + flag (== this Phase-6). Phase 1 = core Document/SDK. Phase 2 = Chart/matplotlib. Phase 3 = validation+fix. Phase 4 = execution tiers + sandbox. Phase 5 = MCP/CLI/GRACE. When speaking about the dev-plan ImplementationOrder, say "Phase-6"; when speaking about the rollout, say "rollout Phase N" or "handover Phase N".
terminology.term-3: "Pure Python Edition" = the entire migration program. "mint_python" = the package path (src/mint_python/). "MP-* modules" = GRACE module IDs registered for that package (currently only MP-PKG). "Python engine" = the runtime choice exposed via MINT_ENGINE=python. Use the most specific term in any given sentence.
terminology.term-4: "M-ENGINE-PYTHON" / "M-ENGINE-JS" are NOT registered GRACE module IDs. The dispatch lives in M-CLI._select_engine + M-CONFIG.engine; there is no separate M-ENGINE module in Phase 0. A real M-ENGINE may be registered in handover Phase 1 when there is code to abstract over.

ArchitectureNotes.followup-1: M-GRACE currently maintains its own zip + Custom XML Parts logic. Once M-OOXML lands, M-GRACE should be refactored to use M-OOXML.unpack/pack so the round-trip and auto-repair invariants apply uniformly. Tracked as a Phase-5+ follow-up wave; not blocking for the Edit pipeline.

contract.purpose: Central configuration for MINT runtime: model endpoint, tier, severity mode, paths, timeouts

fn-loadConfig.PURPOSE: Load from env + .env, validate, return MintConfig (incl. MINT_ENGINE)

type-MintConfig.PURPOSE: frozen dataclass: base_url, model, tier, severity_mode, gotenberg_url, sandbox_timeout, rules_dir, skills_dir, templates_dir, tokens_dir, engine

contract.purpose: Declarative OOXML validation rules loaded from YAML: DOCX hard (D-H01..D-H10), soft (D-S01..D-S05), PPTX hard (P-H01..P-H05)

fn-evaluate.PURPOSE: Evaluate one rule against lxml-parsed document XML, return Violation or None

fn-get_hint.PURPOSE: Return educational reject hint dict for a rule ID

export-all_rules.PURPOSE: Registry: load all YAML rules, return list[Rule]

type-Rule.PURPOSE: dataclass: id, format, severity, xpath, check, fix_category, hint

type-Violation.PURPOSE: dataclass: rule_id, severity, fix_category, message, hint, location

contract.purpose: Run all applicable rules against an OOXML document with severity mode enforcement (audit/lenient/strict)

fn-run_checks.PURPOSE: Execute all rules against document, return ValidationReport with violations grouped by severity

fn-classify_violations.PURPOSE: Group violations: hard_reject, soft_fixable (safe/visual), soft_warn, destructive

export-validate.PURPOSE: Main validation entry point accepting document path + config

type-ValidationReport.PURPOSE: dataclass: violations, total, hard_count, soft_count, mode, passed

contract.purpose: Apply safe and visual auto-fixes to OOXML document with backup; reject destructive; max 3 iterations; detect cascade

fn-apply_fixes.PURPOSE: Iterate: apply safe+visual fixes, re-validate, return FixReport with diffs

fn-create_backup.PURPOSE: Copy document to .bak before any modification

fn-detect_cascade.PURPOSE: Compare violation sets across iterations; abort if not converging

type-FixReport.PURPOSE: dataclass: fixed_path, backup_path, iterations, applied_fixes, remaining_violations, diff

contract.purpose: Compute SHA256 hash of structural style XML for drift detection: styles.xml+numbering.xml (DOCX), theme1.xml (PPTX)

fn-compute.PURPOSE: Extract relevant XML from ZIP, concatenate, SHA256 → hex string

fn-compare.PURPOSE: Compare two hex hashes, return DriftStatus (match/drift/missing)

contract.purpose: Sandboxed Node.js execution of model-generated JS code using docx-js/pptxgenjs/exceljs; no fs/net, 30s timeout

fn-execute.PURPOSE: Pre-check code for forbidden patterns, run in isolated VM, return output file bytes

fn-validate_code.PURPOSE: Static check: reject require('fs'), require('net'), child_process, process.exit, etc.

type-SandboxResult.PURPOSE: dataclass: success, output_path, error, stdout, stderr, duration_ms

contract.purpose: FastMCP server exposing G1 tools: mint_validate, mint_fix, mint_fingerprint as MCP tool calls

fn-mint_validate.PURPOSE: MCP tool: validate document → ValidationReport as JSON

fn-mint_fix.PURPOSE: MCP tool: auto-fix → FixReport as JSON with diff

fn-mint_fingerprint.PURPOSE: MCP tool: compute hash → FingerprintResult as JSON

export-server_g1.PURPOSE: FastMCP server instance with G1 tools registered

contract.purpose: OpenAI-compatible LLM client used by code/plan/section generators and the edit pipeline. Thin httpx wrapper; no streaming, no retries beyond the per-call budget owned by the caller.

fn-call.PURPOSE: POST to /chat/completions, return LLMResponse with text + usage + duration_ms; raise LLMCallError on transport / non-2xx

type-LLMCallError.PURPOSE: Exception base for transport and HTTP errors

M-CLI.NAME: CLI Entry Point

contract.purpose: argparse-based CLI surface for MINT runtime. Subcommands: serve (MCP server), validate, fix, fingerprint, create, extract, and (Phase-5) edit. Each subcommand is a thin shim over the corresponding module entry point and prints JSON to stdout; non-zero exit on logical failure. (Phase-6) Top-level --engine {python,js} flag overrides MINT_ENGINE env; default engine=python raises NotImplementedError via _select_engine until the Pure Python Edition lands.

fn-main.PURPOSE: parse argv, dispatch to cmd_* handler, return exit code

fn-cmd_serve.PURPOSE: start the merged FastMCP G1+G2 server on stdio or sse

fn-cmd_validate.PURPOSE: run M-VALIDATE and emit ValidationReport JSON

fn-cmd_fix.PURPOSE: run M-FIX and emit FixReport JSON

fn-cmd_fingerprint.PURPOSE: run M-FINGERPRINT and emit FingerprintResult JSON

fn-cmd_create.PURPOSE: run M-CREATE and emit CreateResult JSON

fn-cmd_extract.PURPOSE: run M-EXTRACT and emit DesignTokens JSON

fn-cmd_edit.PURPOSE: (Phase-5) load EditPlan from --plan JSON file, run M-EDIT, emit EditResult JSON

fn-_select_engine.PURPOSE: (Phase-6) private chokepoint: returns cfg.engine.value when 'js'; raises NotImplementedError(message naming Phase 0 + remediation) when 'python'. Called once per main() before the cmd_* dispatch dict.

M-CLI.depends: M-CONFIG, M-VALIDATE, M-FIX, M-FINGERPRINT, M-CREATE, M-EXTRACT, M-MCP-G1, M-MCP-G2

contract.purpose: Skill prompt registry: select and render prompts by model tier (small/medium/frontier) and format (docx/pptx); inject design tokens

fn-select_skill.PURPOSE: Lookup prompt file by tier+format from skills/ directory

fn-render_prompt.PURPOSE: Load prompt template, inject design_tokens JSON block, return final prompt string

contract.purpose: Fill master templates (.pptx/.docx) from model-provided JSON content: unpack OOXML ZIP → replace placeholders → apply design tokens → repack

fn-fill.PURPOSE: Unpack template ZIP, replace named placeholders from JSON, apply tokens, repack → output path

fn-list_templates.PURPOSE: Scan builtin/extracted/custom dirs, return template metadata list

contract.purpose: Extract design-tokens.json from existing OOXML documents: Layer 1 (theme/styles.xml = 100% accurate), Layer 2 (statistical layout analysis = ~80%)

fn-parse_theme.PURPOSE: Layer 1: parse theme.xml / styles.xml → colors, typography, dimensions (deterministic)

fn-analyze_layouts.PURPOSE: Layer 2: statistical analysis of slide/page layouts → detected_layouts with frequency

M-CREATE.NAME: Create Orchestrator

contract.purpose: Orchestrate full document generation. Two modes: (1) single-pass: skill → model → execute/template → validate; (2) modular: plan → section[] → assemble → validate. Postprocessor applied after both paths.

fn-create.PURPOSE: Main entry: check request.modular → route to _create_code_mode or _create_modular_mode → postprocess → validate → return CreateResult

fn-_create_modular_mode.PURPOSE: Modular path: M-PLAN.plan() → M-SECTION.generate_all() → M-ASSEMBLE.assemble() → postprocess → validate

fn-_postprocess_docx.PURPOSE: Fix common docx-js/LLM issues: dedup styles, remove empty comments.xml, add tblLayout fixed + tblLook to all tables

type-CreateRequest.PURPOSE: dataclass: format, tier, prompt, design_tokens_path, template_name, model_response_override, modular (bool default False), llm_base_url, llm_api_key, llm_model, self_evaluate, max_refine_rounds

type-CreateResult.PURPOSE: dataclass: output_path (Optional[Path]), validation_report, qa_report, execution_mode (str), duration_ms, error, success, plan (Optional[DocumentPlan]), sections_total (int default 0), sections_succeeded (int default 0)

M-CREATE.depends: M-SKILLS, M-SANDBOX, M-TEMPLATES, M-VALIDATE, M-CONFIG, M-PLAN, M-SECTION, M-ASSEMBLE, M-LLM

M-MCP-G2.NAME: MCP Tools G2

contract.purpose: FastMCP server exposing G1 + G2 tools: mint_create (with modular param), mint_extract_style, mint_list_templates, mint_plan, mint_generate_section, mint_assemble, and (Phase-5) mint_edit for applying typed EditPlans to existing documents.

fn-mint_create.PURPOSE: MCP tool: generate document. If modular=True → plan→section→assemble path. Otherwise single-pass. Returns CreateResult JSON.

fn-mint_plan.PURPOSE: MCP tool: generate DocumentPlan JSON from prompt. Stores plan server-side, returns plan summary. Use for step-by-step modular workflow.

fn-mint_generate_section.PURPOSE: MCP tool: generate code for specified sections of a stored plan. Returns section_id + success status per section. Code stored server-side for assembly.

fn-mint_assemble.PURPOSE: MCP tool: assemble stored plan + section code into final document. Returns output path + AssemblyResult.

fn-mint_extract_style.PURPOSE: MCP tool: extract design tokens → DesignTokens JSON

fn-mint_list_templates.PURPOSE: MCP tool: list available templates → metadata list

fn-mint_edit.PURPOSE: MCP tool (Phase-5): apply an EditPlan JSON to an existing DOCX/PPTX. Inputs: document_path, edit_plan_json, author. Output: EditResult JSON with diff, validation report, output_path. Delegates to M-EDIT.edit; never exposes raw OOXML XML to the model.

export-server_g2.PURPOSE: FastMCP server with G1+G2 tools, mounts G1 server

mcp-state-management.mechanism: In-memory session store (dict[str, MCPGenerationSession]). Each mint_plan call creates a session with a session_id. Subsequent mint_generate_section and mint_assemble calls reference session_id. Session holds: DocumentPlan, dict[str, SectionCode], created_at. Sessions expire after 30 minutes of inactivity.

M-MCP-G2.depends: M-CREATE, M-EXTRACT, M-TEMPLATES, M-PLAN, M-SECTION, M-ASSEMBLE, M-MCP-G1

contract.purpose: Two-level QA: L1 programmatic (rules + XML + fingerprint, <1s) + L2 render-based via Gotenberg (PDF→PNG, overlap/margin/font, 3-5s async)

fn-run_l1.PURPOSE: Programmatic: run validation + fingerprint + XML well-formedness → L1Report (instant)

fn-run_l2.PURPOSE: Render: Gotenberg → PDF → PNG → overlap detection + margin check + font substitution → L2Report (async)

fn-run_qa.PURPOSE: Full QA: L1 sync + L2 async → QAReport with confidence scores (0-1)

contract.purpose: Inject GRACE manifest (structure + fingerprint) and instructions (5-10 rules) as Custom XML Parts into OOXML documents; preserve existing vendor XML

fn-bootstrap.PURPOSE: Inject manifest + instructions into document as Custom XML Parts (urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest)

fn-describe.PURPOSE: Read existing GRACE metadata from document → structure summary

fn-preserve_existing.PURPOSE: Verify non-GRACE Custom XML Parts survive injection unchanged

contract.purpose: Decompose user prompt into a structured DocumentPlan (JSON): sections with types, content descriptions, style constraints, numbering config, header/footer spec. Uses LLM for decomposition but output is small JSON — high success rate on any model.

fn-plan.PURPOSE: Main entry: send decomposition prompt to model → parse JSON → validate → retry on failure → return DocumentPlan

fn-retry_plan.PURPOSE: Re-send prompt with previous error context (invalid JSON, validation errors) up to max_retries

fn-validate_plan.PURPOSE: Check plan: min 1 section, each has id+type+description, numbering refs valid, style IDs standard, page setup valid DXA

fn-render_plan_prompt.PURPOSE: Build system prompt for plan generation with format-specific constraints (DXA widths, standard style IDs, etc.)

type-DocumentPlan.PURPOSE: dataclass: format, sections (list[SectionSpec]), styles (StylesConfig), numbering (list[NumberingConfig]), header_footer (HeaderFooterSpec), page_setup (PageSetup), metadata (PlanMetadata)

type-SectionSpec.PURPOSE: dataclass: id (str), type (str — one of cover|toc|heading|content|table|list|callout|code|images|appendix), title (str), description (str), level (int 1-3), numbering_ref (Optional[str]), column_count (Optional[int] default None), page_break_before (bool default False), skip_header_footer (bool default False), order (int)

type-StylesConfig.PURPOSE: dataclass: colors (dict[str,str] — primary, accent, success, warning, error, text, muted, bg, surface), heading_sizes (dict[str,int] — h1=32, h2=28, h3=24), body_size (int default 22), code_font (str default 'Courier New')

type-NumberingLevel.PURPOSE: dataclass: level (int), format (Literal[bullet|decimal|lower_letter|lower_roman]), text (str), indent_left (int), indent_hanging (int)

type-PageSetup.PURPOSE: dataclass: width (int), height (int), orientation (Literal[portrait|landscape] default portrait), margins (dict[str,int] — top/right/bottom/left, all default 1440)

M-SECTION.NAME: Section Generator
M-SECTION.purpose: Generate docx-js code for a single document section. Each call produces an array of docx-js component calls (Paragraph, Table, etc.) — small enough for 35B models. Section context includes plan structure + sibling titles for coherence.

fn-generate_section.PURPOSE: Build section-specific prompt → send to model → extract JS code → validate → return SectionCode

fn-render_section_prompt.PURPOSE: Build prompt with: section spec from plan, sibling titles/types, shared styles/numbering from plan, sandbox globals, forbidden patterns

fn-render_retry_prompt.PURPOSE: Retry prompt with: original section spec + previous error + previous failed code as negative example

fn-validate_section_code.PURPOSE: Static check: no import/require, no fs, valid JS bracket balance, has expected component patterns

fn-generate_all.PURPOSE: Generate all sections sequentially with sibling context propagation, retry failed up to max_retries per section. Returns GenerateAllResult with success/failure counts.

type-SectionCode.PURPOSE: dataclass: section_id (str), code (str), attempt (int), success (bool), error (Optional[str]), model (str), duration_ms (int)

type-SiblingRef.PURPOSE: dataclass: section_id (str), title (str), type (str) — lightweight reference to sibling section for context

type-GenerateAllResult.PURPOSE: dataclass: sections (dict[str,SectionCode]), total (int), succeeded (int), failed (int), total_retries (int), total_duration_ms (int)

M-ASSEMBLE.NAME: Document Assembler
M-ASSEMBLE.purpose: Assemble final document from DocumentPlan + section code outputs. Pure deterministic JS template — no LLM. Wraps section code into a complete Document constructor with shared styles, numbering config, headers/footers, page setup. Executes via M-SANDBOX.

fn-assemble.PURPOSE: Build complete JS from template + plan config + section code → sandbox execute → postprocess → return AssemblyResult

fn-render_assembly_template.PURPOSE: Generate complete JS: Document({styles, numbering, sections: [Section({properties, children: [/* section code */]})]}) → Packer → writeFileSync

fn-build_styles_config.PURPOSE: Render JS styles config from plan.StylesConfig: docDefaults + paragraphStyles (Heading1-6 with colors/sizes, ListParagraph, Hyperlink) + characterStyles

fn-build_numbering_config.PURPOSE: Render JS numbering config from plan.numbering: each NumberingConfig → {reference, levels: [{format, text, alignment, style}]}

fn-build_headers_footers.PURPOSE: Render JS header/footer from plan.header_footer: Header with PositionalTab for two-column, Footer with PageNumber.CURRENT/TOTAL_PAGES

fn-build_section_wrapper.PURPOSE: Render per-section docx-js Section wrapper: cover=no header/footer, different margins; toc=TableOfContents, content=normal Section. Maps SectionSpec.type to docx-js section configuration.

fn-make_placeholder.PURPOSE: Generate placeholder JS code for failed sections: Paragraph with title + italic error message

type-AssemblyResult.PURPOSE: dataclass: output_path (Optional[Path]), success (bool), sections_included (int), sections_placeholder (int), duration_ms (int), error (Optional[str])

assembly-template-pattern.section-isolation: Each section's code is inserted as a spread array: ...[/* section code */]. If section code defines variables, they're scoped within the section's children array and don't leak to other sections.

contract.purpose: Pure-Python pack/unpack of OOXML containers (DOCX/PPTX/XLSX) for the edit pipeline. Distills Anthropic-style preprocessing (pretty-print, run merge, smart-quote escaping) into deterministic, MIT-clean code with no LibreOffice or shell-out. Round-trip yields tree-equal XML for every part (same tag tree, same attributes) so diffs stay reviewable; binary parts and the [Content_Types].xml override order are preserved byte-for-byte.

fn-unpack.PURPOSE: Extract DOCX/PPTX ZIP into unpack_dir; pretty-print every w:document, w:styles, w:numbering, ppt/slides/*.xml; merge adjacent w:r runs sharing identical w:rPr; escape ASCII single/double quotes inside w:t to smart-quote XML entities; preserve binary parts (media, fontTable.bin, oleObject*.bin) byte-for-byte; return UnpackResult.

fn-pack.PURPOSE: Re-zip unpack_dir into output_path; condense whitespace inserted by unpack while preserving xml:space=preserve runs; auto-repair (regenerate w:durableId >= 0x7FFFFFFF, add xml:space=preserve to w:t with leading/trailing whitespace, normalize 8-hex w:rsid* values); fail-fast on malformed XML, missing relationships, or unknown content types.

fn-merge_runs.PURPOSE: Walk w:p children, fold adjacent w:r with structurally equal w:rPr into one; never merge across w:hyperlink, w:bookmarkStart, w:bookmarkEnd, w:commentRangeStart, w:commentRangeEnd, w:commentReference, w:ins, w:del, w:fldChar, w:tab, w:br, w:sym, or any element introducing a non-text child between the runs.

fn-escape_smart_quotes.PURPOSE: Inside w:t and w:delText only, replace ASCII straight quotes with smart-quote XML entities. Algorithm: per paragraph, alternate opening (“) and closing (”) double quotes starting from opening; replace every ASCII apostrophe with ’. Leave unpaired (odd) double quotes as ASCII. Skip runs whose ancestor pPr/rPr declares a code style or rStyle ending in 'Code'. Idempotent: pre-existing entities are not re-encoded.

fn-validate_relationships.PURPOSE: Cross-check every Target referenced from word/_rels/*.rels and ppt/_rels/*.rels exists in the package; raise OOXML_RELATIONSHIP_BROKEN with a list of dangling targets.

export-ooxml.PURPOSE: Pack/unpack utility instance with default policy from MintConfig

type-UnpackResult.PURPOSE: dataclass: unpack_dir (Path), parts (list[str]), runs_merged (int), quotes_escaped (int), format (Literal['docx','pptx','xlsx']), original_path (Path)

round-trip-invariants.inv-1: unpack(d) → pack(u) → unpack(p) yields identical part listing and identical XML tree per part (modulo intentional pretty-print + run merge + smart-quote normalization).
round-trip-invariants.inv-2: Binary parts under word/media/, ppt/media/, xl/media/, plus fontTable*.bin and oleObject*.bin, are byte-equal between input and output when not edited.
round-trip-invariants.inv-3: Pack never reorders Override entries inside [Content_Types].xml relative to input.

contract.purpose: Edit existing DOCX/PPTX without regeneration. Accepts a declarative EditPlan (JSON list of typed operations) emitted by an LLM and applies it via deterministic XML transforms over an M-OOXML unpack tree. Decouples model output from raw XML — works on 7B-frontier because the model only emits a small typed JSON, never OOXML. Supports tracked changes and comments per ECMA-376.

param.name: severity_mode
param.type: Optional[Literal['audit','lenient','strict']] — defaults to MintConfig.severity_mode when omitted; explicit value overrides config for this call only

fn-edit.PURPOSE: Main entry: validate plan → unpack via M-OOXML → execute operations in order against in-memory lxml tree → pack → run M-VALIDATE → return EditResult with diff and per-op outcomes

fn-validate_plan.PURPOSE: Static check: at least one op, every op.type is one of the supported literals, every op_id is unique within the plan, every anchor.type is supported, every anchor.value is non-empty with length <= 512 and contains no ASCII control characters, every revision op carries author and date

fn-extract_text_with_anchors.PURPOSE: Walk the unpacked tree and emit a list of TextAnchor records: paragraph_index (int, original-tree order), hash (8-hex SHA1 prefix of NFKC-normalized whitespace-collapsed paragraph text), text (the visible plain-text content), part (document/header/footer/footnote/endnote). Used by build_edit_prompt to feed the model and by resolve_anchor at apply time. Never emits OOXML XML — only plain text plus typed anchor metadata.

fn-build_edit_prompt.PURPOSE: Build LLM prompt from user instruction + the TextAnchor list emitted by extract_text_with_anchors + the ECMA-derived op-type schema. Anchors are rendered in a fixed serialization (one TextAnchor per line, prefixed with #<paragraph_index>:<hash>) so the model emits anchor.value matching that exact format. Does NOT show raw XML to the model.

fn-execute_op.PURPOSE: Dispatch one EditOp to its handler: replace_text, insert_paragraph, delete_paragraph, set_paragraph_style, tracked_replace, tracked_delete, add_comment, accept_change, reject_change. Each handler updates the lxml tree in place and returns OpOutcome.

fn-resolve_anchor.PURPOSE: Find unique target run/paragraph/element by Anchor: paragraph_index, text_substring (with optional context_before/after), or stable hash. Resolution semantics: each Anchor maps deterministically to an element-identity captured at unpack time (paragraph_index → original-position, hash → original-paragraph with that hash, text → original substring location). At apply time, M-EDIT looks up that identity in the LIVE tree. If the element is still attached → resolved successfully. If it has been detached or removed by an earlier op in the same plan → EDIT_ANCHOR_NOT_FOUND. Multiple matches surviving without disambiguation → EDIT_ANCHOR_AMBIGUOUS (this includes the case where two different paragraphs share the same 8-hex hash prefix — ambiguity is treated as a hard error, never silently picked).

fn-apply_tracked_change.PURPOSE: Wrap an op's effect into ECMA-376 w:ins/w:del semantics: insertion → wrap new w:r in w:ins(id, author, date); deletion → preserve original w:r inside w:del with w:t→w:delText; full-paragraph delete → also mark paragraph mark via w:pPr/w:rPr/w:del.

fn-add_comment.PURPOSE: Allocate next comment id; append to word/comments.xml; insert w:commentRangeStart/End around target as siblings of w:r (never inside); add w:commentReference run; create comments.xml + comments.xml.rels entries on first use; record reply links in word/commentsExtended.xml via w15:commentEx paraId/paraIdParent (NOT by nesting ranges in document.xml); update word/commentsIds.xml and word/commentsExtensible.xml when present.

fn-render_diff.PURPOSE: Produce a unified human-readable diff: list of (part, op_id, before_snippet, after_snippet) for the EditResult. Truncate snippets to 240 chars and redact via M-CONFIG redaction policy.

type-EditOp.PURPOSE: tagged union discriminated by type (Literal['replace_text','insert_paragraph','delete_paragraph','set_paragraph_style','tracked_replace','tracked_delete','add_comment','accept_change','reject_change']) → variant: ReplaceText | InsertParagraph | DeleteParagraph | SetParagraphStyle | TrackedReplace | TrackedDelete | AddComment | AcceptChange | RejectChange. Every variant carries op_id (str, unique within plan), anchor (Anchor), and a variant-specific payload (e.g. new_text, style_id, author, parent_id, change_id). The discriminator field name 'type' matches Anchor.type so EditPlan JSON has one consistent convention.

type-Anchor.PURPOSE: dataclass: type (Literal['paragraph_index','text','hash']) — discriminator field, value (str|int), context_before (Optional[str]), context_after (Optional[str]), part (Literal['document','header','footer','endnote','footnote'] default 'document'). All three anchor types resolve against the ORIGINAL unpacked tree captured at unpack time; subsequent ops in the same plan do NOT see indices, hashes, or text reshifted by earlier insertions or deletions. type='paragraph_index' carries an int. type='hash' carries an 8-hex-digit SHA1 prefix of the target paragraph's normalized text (whitespace-collapsed, NFKC-normalized) emitted by extract_text_with_anchors so plans stay valid across whitespace-only re-saves. type='text' carries a verbatim substring of the original visible text and may use context_before/context_after for disambiguation. Multi-pass edits that depend on content created by earlier ops are NOT supported in v0 — produce them as a second EditPlan after the first edit has packed and unpacked again.

type-OpOutcome.PURPOSE: dataclass: op_id (str), success (bool), error_code (Optional[str]), affected_part (str), before_snippet (str), after_snippet (str)

type-EditMetadata.PURPOSE: dataclass: correlation_id (str — generated by M-EDIT before the LLM call), source_prompt_hash (str — SHA256 hex of the user instruction text + serialized anchor digest, computed by M-EDIT, NOT supplied by the model), model (str), created_at (str ISO-8601)

type-TextAnchor.PURPOSE: dataclass: paragraph_index (int), hash (str — 8-hex SHA1 prefix of normalized text), text (str), part (Literal['document','header','footer','footnote','endnote'])

type-EditResult.PURPOSE: dataclass: output_path (Optional[Path]), backup_path (Path), success (bool), ops_total (int), ops_succeeded (int), ops_failed (int), validation_report (ValidationReport), diff (list[dict]), duration_ms (int), error (Optional[str])

supported-ops.op-1: In-place text replacement preserving the host w:r's w:rPr; never crosses paragraph boundaries.
supported-ops.op-2: Insert new w:p after anchor with given style id (must exist in styles.xml or come from a standard set: Heading1..6, Normal, ListParagraph).
supported-ops.op-3: Remove anchored w:p; relationships referenced only from this paragraph are cleaned up.
supported-ops.op-4: Change w:pStyle of the anchored paragraph; verify style exists.
supported-ops.op-5: Replace anchored text wrapped in w:del + w:ins siblings, preserving w:rPr on both sides.
supported-ops.op-6: Wrap anchored content in w:del; for full-paragraph deletes also marks the paragraph mark via w:pPr/w:rPr/w:del.
supported-ops.op-7: Insert comment with optional parent_id; updates comments.xml plus relationships and content types; records reply linkage in commentsExtended.xml (w15:commentEx paraIdParent), never by nesting commentRange in document.xml.
supported-ops.op-8: Accept a specific revision by id. For an insertion: remove the w:ins wrapper and keep the inserted runs. For a deletion: drop the w:del subtree (the deleted text is gone). For a paragraph-mark deletion: collapse the paragraph mark.
supported-ops.op-9: Reject a specific revision by id. For an insertion: drop the w:ins subtree (the inserted text is gone). For a deletion: remove the w:del wrapper, restore w:t from w:delText, and keep the runs in place.

out-of-scope-v0.oos-1: Image insertion/replacement (deferred to v0.4 — needs media + relationship + content-type orchestration).
out-of-scope-v0.oos-2: PPTX edit support overall in v0 — the contract is written in DOCX terms (w:p, w:r, w:ins, w:del, comments.xml). PPTX edits, including shape-level edits, are deferred to v0.5 alongside layout-aware anchor resolution. M-EDIT.edit raises EDIT_OP_UNSUPPORTED on any plan with format='pptx' until then.
out-of-scope-v0.oos-3: Bulk accept_all / reject_all of foreign-author revisions via LibreOffice; v0 supports per-id accept_change/reject_change only.

output-policy.pol-1: output_path default: when omitted, M-EDIT writes to the input path with an inserted '.edited' suffix before the extension (foo.docx → foo.edited.docx). An '--in-place' caller flag can request overwriting the original AFTER the backup is on disk.
output-policy.pol-2: backup_path is mandatory and unconditional: M-EDIT copies the original to a sibling path with '.bak' appended BEFORE any tree mutation. If the backup write fails (permissions, disk full), edit aborts with BACKUP_FAILED before unpack.
output-policy.pol-3: output_path and backup_path are always different files. M-EDIT never writes the edited content over the backup.

contract.purpose: Empty parallel package skeleton for the Pure Python Edition. Phase 0 ships only `__init__.py` files for the 8 subpackages mandated by handover §5; subpackage contracts populate in Phases 1-5.

target.source: src/mint_python/__init__.py + 8 subpackage __init__.py files (core, sdk, execution, rules, grace, mcp, cli, tests)
target.tests: tests/unit/test_engine_flag.py (covers import smoke; dedicated MP- test files arrive with future phases)

observability.log-prefix: (none in Phase 0; subpackages will register their own prefixes when contracts land)

contract.purpose: Typography, color, and spacing primitives + named style presets ("alga_corporate" etc.) for the Pure Python Edition. Backbone for every other MP-* core type that emits styled OOXML.

export-Style.PURPOSE: Frozen dataclass: font, size_pt, color_hex, bold, italic, alignment, spacing_before/after, line_height, keep_with_next

export-Pt.PURPOSE: Pt(value) -> int (twentieths of a point); twin of python-docx Pt for downstream conversion

export-load_preset.PURPOSE: Load JSON preset from registry by name OR from path; validate against schema; return populated Style namespace (heading1, body, table_header, caption, ...)

MP-CONTENT.NAME: Pure Python Content Primitives (Paragraph / Run / Image / TabStop)

contract.purpose: Inline content building blocks placed inside a Section: styled text Paragraph (composed of Run instances with optional per-run formatting overrides + link/bookmark anchors) and Image embedded via python-docx + pillow. Direct emitters of `<w:p>`, `<w:r>`, `<w:hyperlink>`, `<w:bookmarkStart/End>`, `<w:drawing>` OOXML.

param.name: run_overrides
param.desc: Per-run override kwargs: bold, italic, underline, color (#RRGGBB hex), font_size_pt (float > 0). None = inherit effective Style.

param.name: run_link
param.desc: External URL or '#anchor' for an internal hyperlink to a bookmark

param.name: run_bookmark
param.desc: Bookmark anchor name (letter/underscore start, alphanumeric tail)

export-Paragraph.PURPOSE: Styled paragraph with optional tab_stops list; .add_run(text, style=None, *, bold/italic/underline/color/font_size_pt/link/bookmark) returns self for chaining; .render(parent_doc) writes to python-docx Document

export-Run.PURPOSE: Inline styled text fragment with optional per-run formatting overrides (bold, italic, underline, color, font_size_pt) + link (external URL or '#anchor') + bookmark (anchor name). Run-field precedence wins over inherited Style at render time.

export-Image.PURPOSE: Image embed: Image.from_path(path, width=None, height=None) | Image.from_bytes(data, format='png'); .render(parent_doc) emits w:drawing with auto rId

export-TabStop.PURPOSE: Frozen dataclass: position_inches (>0) + alignment (TabAlignment) + leader (TabLeader); applied via paragraph_format.tab_stops at render

contract.purpose: Table class with multiple factory constructors (from_list, from_markdown, from_list_of_dicts, financial, comparison), post-construction shaping (apply_style, set_column_widths, autofit), and merged-cells rendering. Cell.colspan/rowspan drive python-docx merge() calls during render; covered cells' content is silently dropped (top-left wins).

param.name: cell_colspan
param.desc: N >= 1 — merge with the next N-1 cells in the same row

param.name: cell_rowspan
param.desc: M >= 1 — merge with the corresponding cells in the next M-1 rows

export-Cell.PURPOSE: Cell value with optional Style override and colspan/rowspan merge directives (validated >= 1 at construction)

fn-from_list.PURPOSE: Table.from_list(rows: list[list]) — first row optional header; Cell(value, colspan=N, rowspan=M) merges at render

fn-financial.PURPOSE: Table.financial(rows) — right-aligns numerics, applies thousands separator

fn-comparison.PURPOSE: Table.comparison(left_label, right_label, rows) — two-column side-by-side preset

contract.purpose: Section fluent surface per handover §3.2: `Section("Title", level=1).add_paragraph(text).add_table(table).add_image(image)`. Phase-1 stubs `add_chart` with NotImplementedError naming Phase 2.

export-Section.PURPOSE: Section node holding a heading + ordered list of content blocks (Paragraph | Table | Image | Chart-stub)

fn-add_paragraph.PURPOSE: Append Paragraph or shorthand str; returns self

fn-add_chart.PURPOSE: STUB — raises NotImplementedError naming Phase 2 (matplotlib chart impl); emits BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD log marker

MP-CALLOUT.NAME: Pure Python Callout Block

contract.purpose: Callout block — visually distinct info / warning / code box rendered as a single-cell python-docx Table with kind-specific shading + thick left accent border + inner cell margins. CODE kind switches to Courier New monospace.

param.name: kind
param.desc: INFO (default) | WARNING | CODE

param.name: title
param.desc: Optional bold heading; None = use kind default ('Info' / 'Warning' / '' for code)

export-Callout.PURPOSE: Callout block: text + kind + optional title; .render(parent_doc) emits a 1x1 python-docx Table styled with kind-specific palette

MP-CALLOUT.depends: (none — direct python-docx + lxml drop-down for tcPr/shd/tcBorders/tcMar)

contract.purpose: List block — bulleted, numbered, or checklist sequence rendered as N python-docx paragraphs with the appropriate built-in list style. Sibling block of Paragraph/Table/Image/Chart inside a Section.

param.name: kind
param.desc: BULLET (default) | NUMBERED | CHECKLIST

param.name: level
param.desc: 0..N nesting depth — applies 0.25 * level inch left indent

export-List.PURPOSE: List block: items + kind + level; .render(parent_doc) emits one styled paragraph per item

contract.purpose: Document facade per handover §3.1: full API surface (cover, sections, TOC, header/footer, save). Stubs `inject_grace`, `validate`, `fix`, `to_pdf` with NotImplementedError pointing at later phases. Save uses python-docx as the backbone; cover (add_picture + WD_BREAK.PAGE) and header/footer (doc.sections[0].header.paragraphs[0].text) use python-docx primitives directly. Only the TOC field uses an lxml drop-down because python-docx has no first-class TOC API.

export-Document.PURPOSE: Document facade aggregating Section list, document-wide Style preset, header/footer, cover

fn-with_style_preset.PURPOSE: Load named preset; returns self for chaining

fn-with_style_from.PURPOSE: Load preset from path; returns self for chaining

fn-add_cover.PURPOSE: Add cover page (title + subtitle + optional logo); rendered via python-docx primitives (add_heading + add_paragraph + add_picture) terminated by a WD_BREAK.PAGE run

fn-add_toc.PURPOSE: Insert table-of-contents field anchor; rendered as Word TOC field with auto-update flag

fn-save.PURPOSE: Serialize to .docx via python-docx + lxml; emits BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX with section_count + table_count + output_path payload

fn-inject_grace.PURPOSE: CORE METHOD with delegation pattern: public surface lives on Document per handover §3.1; implementation delegates to MP-GRACE.inject_manifest_and_instructions(saved_path, manifest, level). Phase-7 STUB raises NotImplementedError naming Phase 5; Phase-5 lands MP-GRACE as a separate module so the Custom XML Parts emission stays decoupled from MP-DOCUMENT.save serialization. Delegation contract: Document.inject_grace(manifest, level=GraceLevel.FULL) → MP-GRACE writes urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest customXml/item*.xml into the saved .docx in-place, preserving all other Custom XML Parts.

fn-validate.PURPOSE: done (Wave-9-4): saves to temp .docx, delegates to MP-VALIDATE.run_checks via temp-file pattern. Returns ValidationReport.

fn-fix.PURPOSE: done (Wave-9-4): saves to temp .docx, delegates to MP-FIX.fix. Returns FixReport.

contract.purpose: Chart class with 7 factory constructors per handover §3.4 (bar, line, stacked_bar, pie, heatmap, waterfall, gantt) + from_matplotlib/from_seaborn/from_plotly channels. Renders matplotlib Figure to PNG @ 300 DPI and embeds via python-docx add_picture; corporate theming applied from active Style preset; chartjunk removal via matplotlib rcParams. Phase-8 unblocks Section.add_chart stub.

export-Chart.PURPOSE: Chart model with cached PNG bytes + width/height/caption + .render(parent_doc)

fn-bar.PURPOSE: Chart.bar(labels, values, title=None, **kwargs) — vertical bar chart

fn-line.PURPOSE: Chart.line(labels, values, title=None, **kwargs) — line chart with optional multi-series

fn-pie.PURPOSE: Chart.pie(labels, values, title=None, **kwargs) — pie chart with corporate palette slices

fn-heatmap.PURPOSE: Chart.heatmap(matrix, row_labels, col_labels, title=None, **kwargs) — matplotlib imshow with colorbar

fn-waterfall.PURPOSE: Chart.waterfall(labels, deltas, title=None, **kwargs) — composed from bar+annotation primitives (matplotlib has no first-class waterfall)

fn-gantt.PURPOSE: Chart.gantt(tasks, title=None, **kwargs) — horizontal bar variant with task spans

fn-from_matplotlib.PURPOSE: Chart.from_matplotlib(fig, caption=None, width_inches=None, vector=True) — wrap user-built matplotlib Figure; vector kwarg accepted for forward-compat but Phase-8 emits PNG @ 300 DPI

fn-from_seaborn.PURPOSE: Chart.from_seaborn(fig, **kwargs) — lazy-imports seaborn; aliases to from_matplotlib (seaborn returns matplotlib Figure)

fn-from_plotly.PURPOSE: Convert plotly Figure to PNG via plotly.io.to_image (kaleido backend, scale=2); lazy-imports plotly — raises ImportError with `pip install plotly kaleido` hint when either dep is missing (Phase-12 unstub commit 7ca3ec3)

fn-render.PURPOSE: Chart.render(parent_doc) — calls parent_doc.add_picture with PNG bytes + Inches(width); emits [MP-Chart][render][BLOCK_RENDER_CHART] chart_type=%s emu_width=%d

contract.purpose: Public re-exports of all §3 types so users write `from mint_python.sdk import Document, Section, Table, Style, Image, TOC, Pt, ColorPalette`. Hosts the named-preset registry and the with_style_from(path) loader.

error.code: (none — re-export module; underlying modules raise their own errors)

MP-RULES.NAME: Pure Python OOXML Rule Engine
MP-RULES.purpose: Load OOXML validation rules from shared YAML (rules/*.yaml) and evaluate via lxml XPath. Pure Python successor to M-RULES.

MP-VALIDATE.NAME: Pure Python Validation Engine
MP-VALIDATE.purpose: Open saved OOXML documents, parse XML via lxml, run all MP-RULES, classify violations, produce ValidationReport. Pure Python successor to M-VALIDATE.

MP-FIX.NAME: Pure Python Auto-Fix Engine
MP-FIX.purpose: Apply safe/visual auto-fixes to OOXML documents with backup, cascade detection, destructive rejection. Pure Python successor to M-FIX.
MP-FIX.depends: MP-VALIDATE, MP-RULES, zipfile, lxml, shutil, hashlib, io

MP-MD-ADAPTER.NAME: Markdown → ArticleSpec walker

contract.purpose: Convert raw markdown text into an ArticleSpec dataclass deterministically (no LLM). Uses markdown-it-py 4.2 with the gfm-like preset to parse the source into a token tree, then walks the tree producing typed blocks (paragraph, callout, list, table, code) and SpecSections matching the existing tools/article_experiment/spec.ArticleSpec contract. Exists because LLM-driven structuring of dense markdown (especially tables) is unreliable on non-frontier models — confirmed empirically when the Round-3-postfix baseline_report.docx came out of a 4B model with tables flattened into parallel paragraphs. Markdown is a structured input; structure should be extracted by code, not interpreted by a model.

param.name: md_text
param.desc: GitHub-Flavored Markdown source; tolerant to malformed input

param.name: title_override
param.desc: Optional title; otherwise derived from first H1 or fallback

export-markdown_to_spec.PURPOSE: fn-markdown_to_spec(md_text, *, title_override=None) -> ArticleSpec; deterministic walker; emits [MP-MdAdapter][parse][BLOCK_PARSE_MD] at INFO

export-MarkdownAdapterError.PURPOSE: Base error for adapter-level failures (empty input, no usable content)

MP-MD-ADAPTER.depends: markdown-it-py (4.2, new dep), tools/article_experiment/spec (ArticleSpec, SpecSection, SectionLayout, ParagraphBlock, CalloutBlock, ListBlock, TableBlock, CodeBlock — types reused, not duplicated)

MP-MCP-AUDIT.NAME: MCP server inventory + migration plan (design-only)

contract.purpose: Design-only deliverable: read existing src/mint/mcp_g1.py and src/mint/mcp_g2.py, inventory all FastMCP @mcp.tool registrations, classify each tool (stateful / stateless, elicitation-aware / one-shot, kept / migrate / deprecate), perform gap analysis against the new product shape (design-tokens-as-resources, versioned templates, GRACE-as-audit-trail, elicitation-driven planning), and produce a migration plan. Output is docs/mcp-audit.md as a human-readable architecture review — no runtime code in this module. The doc becomes the brief for Phase-14 (actual migration). Audit-only scope is deliberate: keeps research separate from engineering.

param.name: legacy_servers
param.desc: Read-only inputs

param.name: audit_doc
param.desc: Sectioned report: inventory, state analysis, gap analysis, migration plan, new-tool roadmap

export-none.PURPOSE: Design artifact only — no Python module exposes anything; the deliverable is docs/mcp-audit.md

MP-MCP-AUDIT.depends: src/mint/mcp_g1.py (read), src/mint/mcp_g2.py (read), src/mint/llm.py (read for client patterns)

MP-MEMO-POC.NAME: Memo MCP tool with FastMCP elicitation (planning-mode wow-demo)

contract.purpose: End-to-end POC of the planning-mode dialog pattern via the MCP elicitation primitive (spec 2025-06-18, FastMCP Context.elicit). Exposes one tool, create_memo(intent, source_md?, ctx), that: (1) parses intent + optional markdown source via MP-MD-ADAPTER, (2) builds a partial MemoSpec, (3) for each missing required field, calls ctx.elicit(...) which the connected MCP client (Claude Desktop primary; OpenWebUI / Cursor / Copilot follow) renders as a structured form to the end user; (4) once all 5 required fields present, builds a Document via MP-DOCUMENT with the klawd preset and a memo-specific YAML template (templates/memo.yaml); (5) injects a GRACE manifest with audit-trail metadata (model identity from session, timestamp, slot provenance); (6) returns the saved docx path and the audit-trail id. Memo template is a YAML data file (per "design as data, not code" rule), not embedded in Python — sets the precedent for future doc types (Letter, Report, Contract).

param.name: intent
param.desc: Free-text user intent describing the memo to generate

param.name: source_md
param.desc: Optional markdown source (e.g. a chat transcript) parsed via MP-MD-ADAPTER for fact extraction

param.name: ctx
param.desc: FastMCP context exposing elicit() / sample() / progress notifications

param.name: result
param.desc: {path: str, audit_id: str, fields_elicited: list[str]} — path to saved docx, GRACE audit id, list of field names that required elicitation

error.desc: User declined to provide a required field via the MCP client; tool returns error without producing docx
memo.yaml template missing on disk
Builder failed at render time (e.g. invalid spec after elicitation)

export-create_memo.PURPOSE: @mcp.tool async fn-create_memo(intent, source_md, ctx) -> dict; main entry; emits [MP-Memo][create][BLOCK_PARSE_INTENT], [BLOCK_ELICIT_FIELD], [BLOCK_BUILD_DOCX], [BLOCK_INJECT_GRACE]

type-MemoSpec.PURPOSE: frozen dataclass with 5 required fields: sender, recipient, date, subject, body. POC scope deliberately tight — extends in Phase-14+ with cc, signature, classification, distribution_list

type-MemoTemplate.PURPOSE: Loader for templates/memo.yaml — declares field order, layout (header / body / signature blocks), klawd-derived styling refinements

MP-MEMO-POC.depends: MP-MD-ADAPTER (parse source_md), MP-DOCUMENT (render docx), MP-STYLE (klawd preset application — apply_preset_to_doc), MP-GRACE (audit manifest injection), fastmcp (Context.elicit primitive), pyyaml (read templates/memo.yaml)

MP-GRACE.NAME: Pure Python GRACE Injector v1.1

contract.purpose: Inject GRACE manifest + 10 instruction rules as Custom XML Parts under urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest into a saved .docx, preserving any existing customXml entries from other vendors. Pure Python successor to M-GRACE; stdlib-only deps. Called via delegation from MP-DOCUMENT.inject_grace.

type-GRACEManifest.PURPOSE: Manifest dataclass: document_structure dict, fingerprint hash, instructions list, namespace, xml_part_name

type-GRACEInjectionError.PURPOSE: Raised on path-traversal in source ZIP, missing source file, or structure analysis failure

fn-bootstrap.PURPOSE: Inject GRACE manifest + instructions Custom XML Part into source .docx; emits [MP-Grace][inject][BLOCK_INJECT_MANIFEST] at INFO; returns GRACEManifest

fn-describe.PURPOSE: Read existing GRACE manifest from .docx if present (looks for grace/*.xml); returns GRACEManifest or None

MP-GRACE.depends: none (stdlib only — zipfile, xml.etree.ElementTree, hashlib, uuid, tempfile, logging)

MP-DOC-GENERIC.NAME: Generic create_document MCP tool (replaces hardcoded create_memo)

contract.purpose: Lift the Phase-13 MEMO-POC pipeline out of its hardcoded "memo" shape into a generic create_document(intent, doc_type, source_md?, ctx) tool. Document type is selected at call-time from the templates/ registry; the pipeline (heuristic field extraction → ctx.elicit with chat-driven fallback → MP-DOCUMENT build with klawd preset → MP-GRACE audit injection) is the same for every doc_type. Adding Letter / Report / Contract / Invoice becomes a templates/<name>.yaml file with no Python changes.

param.name: intent
param.desc: Free-text user intent

param.name: doc_type
param.desc: Template name (memo, letter, report, contract, ...) — must match an entry in templates/

param.name: source_md
param.desc: Optional markdown source for fact extraction via MP-MD-ADAPTER

param.name: result
param.desc: Triple-shape: TextContent (markdown link) + ResourceLink (file://) + structured_content {status, path, audit_id, fields_elicited, doc_type, template_version}

error.desc: No template matching doc_type in templates/
User declined elicit
Builder error after fields collected

export-create_document.PURPOSE: @server.tool async fn-create_document; replaces create_memo as the production entry-point

type-DocumentSpec.PURPOSE: Generic dataclass driven by template required_fields; replaces hardcoded MemoSpec

MP-DOC-GENERIC.depends: MP-MEMO-POC (refactor source), MP-MD-ADAPTER, MP-DOCUMENT, MP-STYLE, MP-GRACE, MP-TEMPLATES-REGISTRY (template lookup), fastmcp.Context, pyyaml

target.source: src/mint_python/mcp/document.py (replaces mcp/memo.py; memo.py becomes a 5-line backwards-compat alias)
target.tests: tests/integration/test_mp_doc_generic.py + retire tests/integration/test_mp_memo_poc.py once parity is proven

MP-TEMPLATES-REGISTRY.NAME: Template registry + read MCP tools (list_templates, get_template)

contract.purpose: Read-only registry over templates/ — discovers YAML files (and their semver-versioned siblings), validates against the TemplateSchema, exposes them through MCP tools. First half of audit's Priority-2 (templates as discoverable resources).

param.name: templates_dir
param.desc: Default ./templates; override via MINT_TEMPLATES_DIR env

param.name: list_templates_result
param.desc: (name, version, doc_type, description, last_modified) per discovered template

param.name: get_template_result
param.desc: Full parsed YAML as dict for the requested name+version

export-Registry.PURPOSE: TemplateRegistry — walks templates/, parses + validates YAML files; returns list/get queries

export-get_template.PURPOSE: @server.tool async fn-get_template(name, version='latest', ctx); returns full YAML content

type-TemplateSchema.PURPOSE: Generic schema replacing MemoTemplate; required_fields + layout + doc_type + version + description

target.source: src/mint_python/templates/registry.py + templates/letter.yaml (new doc_type to demonstrate generality) + templates/_README.md (template authoring guide)

MP-TEMPLATES-WRITE.NAME: Template versioning + write tool (cross-model handoff)

contract.purpose: Versioned write side of the template registry. update_template tool authors a new semver-bumped YAML file; never overwrites existing versions. Sidecar audit log records every write. Closes audit's Priority-4 — frontier model edits skeleton in low-sensitivity context, local model later consumes the new version on sensitive data, produced docx's GRACE manifest names the lineage.

param.name: content
param.desc: Full YAML content to write

param.name: author
param.desc: Identity recorded in audit log + GRACE manifest

param.name: result
param.desc: {name, version (bumped), audit_id, predecessor_version, written_to (path)}

error.desc: Concurrent write to the same target version

export-update_template.PURPOSE: @server.tool async fn-update_template(name, content, author, ctx); bumps semver + writes new file + appends audit-log entry

type-TemplateAuditEntry.PURPOSE: Sidecar JSON Lines record per write: {timestamp, name, version, predecessor_version, author, content_sha256}

MP-TEMPLATES-WRITE.depends: MP-TEMPLATES-REGISTRY (validation + version detection), MP-GRACE (manifest extension carries template lineage), pyyaml

MP-MCP-RESOURCES.NAME: MCP resources for templates + presets (list_presets / get_preset, mint:// URI scheme)

contract.purpose: Wire FastMCP @server.resource handlers exposing mint://template/<name> and mint://preset/<name> URI schemes. Clients that prefer the resources/list + resources/read protocol over tool-calls (Cursor, VS Code Insiders MCP) get native enumeration without per-template tool calls. Symmetric tools list_presets / get_preset land for clients that don't support the resources protocol (current Claude Desktop). Closes audit's Priority-1.

param.name: uri
param.desc: mint://template/<name> or mint://preset/<name>

param.name: resource_content
param.desc: MIME-typed YAML or JSON content of the requested template/preset

export-resource_handlers.PURPOSE: FastMCP @server.resource handlers for mint://template/* and mint://preset/* URIs

export-get_preset.PURPOSE: @server.tool fn-get_preset(name, ctx); returns full YAML/JSON preset content

MP-AUTH-SHIM.NAME: Authorization shim for write-path MCP tools (template-writer allowlist)

contract.purpose: Production-blocker fix: today update_template is writable by ANY connected MCP client (after Phase-14 that includes any local model attached via Cursor / OpenWebUI / sidecar). This shim gates write-path tools behind a config-driven author allowlist; read tools (list_templates / get_template / mint:// resources) stay open. First write tool gated is update_template; future write tools route through the same shim. Closes the audit's Priority-4 sub-clause "Authorization shim: read = open; write = config-gated allowlist" left as TODO at Phase-14 close.

param.name: author
param.desc: Author identity supplied by the calling MCP tool (typically from ctx or explicit param)

param.name: env_MINT_TEMPLATE_WRITERS
param.desc: Comma-separated allowlist; takes precedence over file config

param.name: config_file
param.desc: ~/.config/mint/writers.json containing {writers: [str, ...]}; loaded on first call

param.name: is_writer
param.desc: True if author is on the allowlist or open-mode is active

error.desc: Author is not on the allowlist; raised before any disk mutation
writers.json malformed or env value uninterpretable; raised at registry load, not at call time

export-is_template_writer.PURPOSE: fn-is_template_writer(author: str) -> bool — pure check, no side effects

export-require_template_writer.PURPOSE: fn-require_template_writer(author: str) -> None — raises TEMPLATE_WRITE_FORBIDDEN on deny; called by update_template before any disk write

export-load_writers_config.PURPOSE: fn-load_writers_config() -> WritersConfig — reads MINT_TEMPLATE_WRITERS env then config file; cached per-process; emits BLOCK_AUTH_OPEN_MODE warning once if empty

type-WritersConfig.PURPOSE: frozen dataclass: writers tuple[str, ...], source Literal['env','file','none'], open_mode bool

MP-AUTH-SHIM.depends: stdlib only (os, json, pathlib, dataclasses, logging). No coupling with MP-TEMPLATES-WRITE; consumer-side import keeps the shim independently testable.

MP-MANIFEST-READ.NAME: GRACE manifest read tool (mint_read_grace_manifest)

contract.purpose: Symmetric read counterpart to GRACE injection. Exposes the mint_read_grace_manifest MCP tool that opens any docx, locates grace/manifest_*.xml, and returns the parsed manifest as a dict. Closes audit Priority-1 acceptance criterion "New tool read_grace_manifest(document_path) exists and round-trips a manifest written by any other tool" — explicitly named in audit §1 row-7, §3(c), and §5 roadmap, but never shipped during Phase-14 (Wave-2 only added inject; the read side was deferred). Useful UX for "verify provenance of this docx I received" (cross-model handoff scenario where the receiving model wants to inspect what generated the file).

param.name: document_path
param.desc: Path to a .docx file

param.name: manifest
param.desc: {audit_id, model_identity, fields_elicited, template, template_version, template_author, timestamp, instructions, fingerprint, namespace}

error.desc: docx has no urn:mint:grace:2026:manifest custom XML part
manifest XML present but unparseable
path is not a valid .docx zip / path traversal rejected

export-mint_read_grace_manifest.PURPOSE: @server.tool async fn-mint_read_grace_manifest(document_path, ctx) — thin wrapper around MP-GRACE.describe; canonicalizes the dict shape and surfaces tool-level errors

MP-MANIFEST-READ.depends: MP-GRACE (existing fn-describe), M-SECURITY safe_doc helper for path-traversal guard, fastmcp.Context

MP-VISUAL-QA-HOOK.NAME: Visual QA hook on create_document (VLM rubric)

contract.purpose: Advisory post-create_document visual quality gate. After every successful create_document (and the create_memo alias), run the produced docx through the existing VLM rubric scorer (typography / layout / visual_richness / professional axes via qwen2.5vl:7b on Ollama). Score is attached to the tool result's structured_content as visual_qa: {score, axes, issues, advisory: true}; the tool NEVER fails on visual-QA outcome. Defaults ON; opt out via MINT_SKIP_VISUAL_QA=1. Closes the structure-vs-visual gap at Phase-14 close: MP-VALIDATE checks OOXML structure but never confirms the rendered doc visually matches the preset's intent.

param.name: document_path
param.desc: Saved docx path (after MP-GRACE injection)

param.name: preset_name
param.desc: Active preset (currently always 'klawd' but parameterized for future presets)

param.name: env_MINT_SKIP_VISUAL_QA
param.desc: When '1' the hook short-circuits and structured_content omits visual_qa key

param.name: env_MINT_VISUAL_QA_PAGES
param.desc: Max pages to score; default 5

param.name: report
param.desc: None when skipped or backend unavailable; structured score otherwise

error.desc: soffice / pdftoppm / Ollama unreachable; logged as warning, hook returns None, tool succeeds

export-score_document.PURPOSE: fn-score_document(path, preset_name='klawd') -> VisualQAReport | None — refactor of root-level e2e_qa_visual.py into a library function, with backend-unavailable degradation built in

type-VisualQAReport.PURPOSE: frozen dataclass: score int (0-100), axes dict[str, int], issues list[str], pages_scored int, preset_name str, advisory bool=True, skipped bool=False, skip_reason str | None

MP-VISUAL-QA-HOOK.depends: soffice (LibreOffice headless) for docx→PDF, pdftoppm (poppler-utils) for PDF→PNG, Ollama VLM endpoint (qwen2.5vl:7b), pillow for PNG reads, urllib.request stdlib. Hook integrated by MP-DOC-GENERIC.create_document via post-step in src/mint_python/mcp/document.py.

target.source: src/mint_python/qa/visual.py + edits to src/mint_python/mcp/document.py (~10 LOC hook call) + retire root-level e2e_qa_visual.py (replaced by library + a 5-line CLI shim if still needed)

MP-LEGACY-DEPRECATION.NAME: Remove mcp_g1/mcp_g2 + JS sandbox path + legacy create/assemble pipeline

contract.purpose: Subtractive cleanup. Honors audit §6 "Tools to deprecate" and the unmet Phase-14 promise to "deprecate JS sandbox in W1, remove in W3" — neither happened during Phase-14. Phase-15 fulfils both at once: the legacy mcp_g1 / mcp_g2 surfaces + the MINT_ENGINE=js code path + the docx-js / pptxgenjs / exceljs Node deps are removed entirely. Pre-flight audit on 2026-05-10 (controller-side, before Wave-15-1 dispatch) revealed deeper coupling: mint.create:47-48 + mint.assemble:616-617 import mint.sandbox directly; mint.cli:44-45 mounts mcp_g1/mcp_g2 in cmd_serve. Scope expanded: also delete mint.create + mint.assemble (legacy JS-tier orchestration superseded by mint_python.mcp.document.create_document since Phase-14) and the mint serve CLI subcommand mount path. Pure-python (mint_python) becomes the only execution surface. Per memory project_mint_v2 + requirements.xml constraint-7, this is the strategic direction; Phase-15 is the cleanup that finally ships it.

param.name: N/A
param.desc: No runtime inputs; this is a code/dep removal step

param.name: N/A
param.desc: No new exports; net public surface SHRINKS by 5 legacy MCP tools (mint_create, mint_fix, mint_validate, mint_extract_style, mint_fingerprint as exposed via mcp_g1/mcp_g2) and the --engine js CLI flag

error.desc: No new error codes; legacy import sites get ModuleNotFoundError pointing at mint_python successors

removal-mcp_g1.PURPOSE: DELETE src/mint/mcp_g1.py — registers mint_validate / mint_fix / mint_fingerprint on FastMCP('MINT-G1'); successors are mint_python.mcp.* tools already shipped in Phase-14

removal-mcp_g2.PURPOSE: DELETE src/mint/mcp_g2.py — registers mint_create / mint_extract_style / mint_list_templates / mint_edit on FastMCP('MINT-G2'); mint_create_document / mint_list_templates (Python) / mint_edit (TBD migration) supersede

removal-sandbox.PURPOSE: DELETE src/mint/sandbox/ — Node.js vm2/isolated-vm sandbox; pure-python runtime makes it dead code

removal-create.PURPOSE: DELETE src/mint/create.py (~120 LOC) — legacy JS-tier orchestrator (M-CREATE); imports mint.sandbox directly at line 47-48; superseded by mint_python.mcp.document.create_document since Phase-14. Scope-expansion 2026-05-10 (pre-flight discovery).

removal-assemble.PURPOSE: DELETE src/mint/assemble.py (~700 LOC) — legacy JS-tier modular generator (M-ASSEMBLE); imports mint.sandbox at line 616-617; only used by mint.create which is also being removed. Scope-expansion 2026-05-10.

removal-cmd_serve.PURPOSE: REMOVE the cmd_serve subcommand from src/mint/cli.py (which mounts mcp_g1 + mcp_g2 via FastMCP('MINT').mount(g1).mount(g2) at lines 44-45). The MCP server entry point becomes the FastMCP server in src/mint_python/mcp/document.py invoked directly via stdio in claude_desktop_config.json. The 'mint serve' CLI surface either disappears entirely or repoints at the pure-python server. Scope-expansion 2026-05-10.

removal-engine_js.PURPOSE: REMOVE the Engine.JS branch from src/mint/cli.py::_select_engine; --engine js arg either removed or kept as deprecated stub that exits with a pointer to mint_python tools

removal-deps.PURPOSE: REMOVE docx (9.x), pptxgenjs (3.x), exceljs (4.x), vm2/isolated-vm sandbox runtime hint, eslint, vitest from pyproject.toml + uv.lock; update docs/technology.xml Runtime + Dependencies + Tooling sections; update docs/requirements.xml constraint-7 to reflect removal (was 'preserved as optional fallback' → now 'removed entirely')

removal-tests.PURPOSE: DELETE tests/integration/test_mcp_g1.py, tests/integration/test_mcp_g2.py, tests/unit/test_sandbox.py, any tests/unit/test_create.py + tests/unit/test_assemble.py + tests/integration/test_create*.py + test_assemble*.py targeting the deleted modules; tests/integration/test_cli.py loses --engine js + cmd_serve branch coverage

MP-LEGACY-DEPRECATION.depends: Pre-flight audit completed 2026-05-10 (controller-side, before Wave-15-1 dispatch): tools/article_experiment/ + scripts/ are clean (no legacy imports). Legacy importers found: src/mint/create.py:47-48 (sandbox), src/mint/assemble.py:616-617 (sandbox), src/mint/cli.py:44-45 (mcp_g1+mcp_g2). All 3 in-tree importers are themselves being deleted in this expanded scope; no stub-shim required. Update src/mint/__init__.py + pyproject entry points if mcp_g1/g2/create/assemble were referenced.

target.source: src/mint/mcp_g1.py (delete) + src/mint/mcp_g2.py (delete) + src/mint/sandbox/ (delete recursive) + src/mint/create.py (delete; scope-expanded) + src/mint/assemble.py (delete; scope-expanded) + src/mint/cli.py (edit: drop cmd_serve + Engine.JS dispatch) + src/mint/config.py (drop Engine.JS) + pyproject.toml (drop deps + extras + entry points referencing deleted modules) + docs/technology.xml (Runtime/Deps/Tooling sections) + docs/requirements.xml (constraint-7) + .env.example (drop MINT_ENGINE example)

MP-MCP-VALIDATE.NAME: mint_validate_document MCP tool (Phase-16 A1)

contract.purpose: Closes the MCP-parity gap left by Phase-15: external clients can validate caller-supplied .docx files through the same governed surface that produces them. Thin wrapper over the existing pure-python successor `mint_python.validate.validate`. Read-only; no allowlist (validation reads bytes, never mutates). Closes audit §6 row "mint_validate (legacy mcp_g1, deleted Phase-15) — successor pending".

param.name: document_path
param.desc: Path to a .docx or .pptx file the caller wants validated

param.name: severity_mode
param.desc: Strictness; defaults to 'lenient' to match Document.validate

param.name: report
param.desc: Canonical ValidationReport dict: {passed: bool, severity_mode, violations: [{rule_id, severity, message, hint, location}], counts: {hard, soft, total}, format}

error.desc: Path not a .docx/.pptx zip OR path traversal rejected by safe_doc
rules dir not found OR lxml parse error before any rule evaluation; carries the failing rule_id or 'XML-001'

export-mint_validate_document.PURPOSE: @server.tool async fn-mint_validate_document(document_path, severity_mode='lenient', ctx) — calls mint_python.validate.validate; canonicalizes ValidationReport dataclass to dict; surfaces structured errors with no traceback

fn-_canonicalize_report.PURPOSE: ValidationReport dataclass → dict with stable key order; severity_mode echoed for evidence

MP-MCP-VALIDATE.depends: mint_python.validate.validate (existing), mint._security.safe_doc (path-traversal guard), fastmcp.Context + ToolError. MUST NOT import any deleted-Phase-15 surface.

MP-MCP-FIX.NAME: mint_fix_document MCP tool (Phase-16 A2)

contract.purpose: Closes the MCP-parity gap for the auto-fix surface. Thin wrapper over `mint_python.fix.fix`. Mutates the caller-supplied .docx in place; preserves the existing backup-before-write invariant (a .bak file lands beside the original on every fix call). No allowlist by design — the caller owns the doc path. Read-path auth contract from V-MP-AUTH-SHIM forbidden-1 explicitly extends to this tool: MUST NOT consult MP-AUTH-SHIM (it's caller-doc, not template/preset).

param.name: document_path
param.desc: Path to a .docx the caller wants fixed in place

param.name: severity_mode
param.desc: Severity threshold for fix decisions; defaults to 'lenient'

param.name: max_iterations
param.desc: Cascade cap; defaults to 3 (matches mint_python.fix.fix default)

param.name: report
param.desc: Canonical FixReport dict: {applied_fixes, remaining_violations, backup_path, iterations, diff_summary, severity_mode}

error.desc: Path not a .docx zip
One or more violations classified destructive; short-circuit before mutation
Could not write .bak (permission / disk full); raised before any in-place mutation
No convergence after max_iterations

export-mint_fix_document.PURPOSE: @server.tool async fn-mint_fix_document(document_path, severity_mode='lenient', max_iterations=3, ctx) — calls mint_python.fix.fix; canonicalizes FixReport; surfaces structured errors

fn-_canonicalize_fix_report.PURPOSE: FixReport dataclass → dict with stable key order

MP-MCP-FIX.depends: mint_python.fix.fix (existing), mint_python.validate (re-validation), mint._security.safe_doc, fastmcp.Context + ToolError. MUST NOT consult MP-AUTH-SHIM.

MP-FINGERPRINT.NAME: Pure-Python fingerprint module (Phase-16 A3 port)

contract.purpose: Port `src/mint/fingerprint.py` (~150 LOC) to `src/mint_python/fingerprint.py` so the MCP wrap layer can avoid the constraint-8 violation of `from mint.fingerprint import …`. Preserves the exact public API of the legacy module (compute / compare / fingerprint / FingerprintResult / DriftStatus / FingerprintError hierarchy). Pure stdlib + lxml. The legacy module stays in place — Phase-16 is additive, NOT a delete-and-replace. Phase-17 (or later) can retire the legacy copy.

param.name: DriftStatus

I/O or hash backend failure mid-read
Base class

export-fingerprint.PURPOSE: fn-fingerprint(document_path) — public entry; thin wrapper over compute() returning FingerprintResult

export-compute.PURPOSE: fn-compute(document_path) -> FingerprintResult — opens zip, reads styles.xml, SHA-256 over chunked content

export-compare.PURPOSE: fn-compare(hash_a, hash_b) -> DriftStatus — pure equality check; None inputs → UNKNOWN

MP-DOC-BUNDLE.NAME: Phase-16 doc_types bundle (report, ADR, contract, bilingual NDA)

contract.purpose: Pure-data module: adds four new YAML doc_types to `templates/` so the existing MP-DOC-GENERIC pipeline can render them with zero Python changes. Demonstrates that the generic create_document pipeline is doc-type agnostic in the Phase-14 W1 sense — different required_fields drive different elicitation order, different layout shapes ship per file. Closes the OpenQuestion "minimum viable set of builtin templates for launch" (requirements.xml). For O!Bank demo: report covers product reviews, ADR covers product decisions, contract covers vendor onboarding, bilingual NDA covers Ru/En cross-border deals.

param.name: N/A
param.desc: No runtime inputs; pure data ship

param.name: report.yaml
param.desc: doc_type='report'; required_fields=(title, author, date, summary, sections, conclusions); layout walks each section as heading + body

param.name: decision-record.yaml
param.desc: doc_type='decision-record'; required_fields=(title, author, date, status, context, decision, consequences); layout = ADR template

param.name: contract.yaml
param.desc: doc_type='contract'; required_fields=(parties, effective_date, term, scope, obligations, signatures); layout = formal contract shape

param.name: nda-bilingual-ru-en.yaml
param.desc: doc_type='nda-bilingual-ru-en'; required_fields=(party_a, party_b, effective_date, scope_ru, scope_en, term_ru, term_en, signatures); layout walks paired Ru/En clauses (stacked: each clause = [Ru paragraph, En paragraph, spacer]). No engine extension; engine renders the same heading/spacer/paragraph primitives.

error.desc: No new error codes; existing MP-TEMPLATES-REGISTRY validation catches malformed YAML at registry load

yaml-nda-bilingual.PURPOSE: templates/nda-bilingual-ru-en.yaml — stacked Ru/En NDA; clause bodies are 2-paragraph blocks (Ru then En)

MP-DOC-BUNDLE.depends: MP-DOC-GENERIC (the create_document pipeline that consumes these templates), MP-TEMPLATES-REGISTRY (schema validator). No Python code changes; the bundle is consumed by existing infrastructure.

MP-THEME-EDIT.NAME: Structured preset editor MCP tools (palette/typography/spacing)

contract.purpose: Ships three structured-patch MCP tools so a model can edit a preset's design tokens without composing valid YAML by hand. Each tool takes a structured patch object, validates it, re-emits a versioned preset YAML, and writes it as `presets/<name>_v<next-semver>.yaml` (mirroring MP-TEMPLATES-WRITE versioning). All three gate through MP-AUTH-SHIM.require_template_writer — themes are sub-noun of templates per Phase-16 framing; the MINT_TEMPLATE_WRITERS allowlist applies. Sidecar audit log `presets/_audit.jsonl` records each write. The existing `mint_list_presets` / `mint_get_preset` read tools (Phase-14 MP-MCP-RESOURCES) are reused unchanged.

param.name: name
param.desc: Preset name (e.g. 'klawd', 'claret_serif', 'alga_corporate')

param.name: palette
param.desc: palette tool: {primary?, secondary?, accent?, text?, background?} hex strings; missing keys preserve existing values

param.name: typography
param.desc: typography tool: {heading_font?, body_font?, base_size_pt?, heading_scale?, line_height?}

param.name: spacing
param.desc: spacing tool: {paragraph_pt?, section_pt?, page_margin_top_pt?, page_margin_side_pt?}

param.name: author
param.desc: Author identity routed through MP-AUTH-SHIM

param.name: result
param.desc: {name, version, audit_id, predecessor_version, patched_fields: [...]}

error.desc: No preset by that name in core/presets/ (read base resolves nothing)
Author not on MINT_TEMPLATE_WRITERS allowlist; raised by MP-AUTH-SHIM before any disk mutation
Patch payload has wrong shape (e.g. hex not parseable, font name empty, negative spacing)
Concurrent write attempted; version bump collision (matches MP-TEMPLATES-WRITE pattern)

export-mint_update_preset_palette.PURPOSE: @server.tool async fn-mint_update_preset_palette(name, palette, author, ctx) — calls require_template_writer; merges palette into existing preset; re-emits versioned YAML

fn-_apply_patch.PURPOSE: pure dict-merge helper; missing patch keys preserve existing preset values; validates ranges (positive spacing, hex format, etc.)

fn-_bump_preset_version.PURPOSE: reads max version among `presets/<name>_v*.yaml`, returns next semver minor bump (mirrors MP-TEMPLATES-WRITE._bump_template_version)

fn-_append_preset_audit.PURPOSE: appends one JSONL line to presets/_audit.jsonl: {timestamp, name, version, author, predecessor_version, patched_fields, content_sha256}

MP-THEME-EDIT.depends: MP-AUTH-SHIM (require_template_writer; reuses MINT_TEMPLATE_WRITERS allowlist), MP-STYLE (BUILTIN_PRESETS for base read), MP-MCP-RESOURCES (mint:// read URIs continue to surface the new versions), pyyaml, fastmcp.Context + ToolError.

MP-EDIT.NAME: Pure-Python edit pipeline port (Phase-16 A5 port)

contract.purpose: Port `src/mint/edit.py` (~2003 LOC) to `src/mint_python/edit.py` — the largest port of the Pure Python Edition cycle. Preserves the legacy M-EDIT public surface verbatim: EditPlan / EditOp / TextAnchor / OpOutcome dataclasses + edit() entrypoint + resolve_anchor() + make_edit_metadata() + apply_tracked_change + comment handlers. Section-by-section port plan: (1) anchor resolver + extract_text_with_anchors (~500 LOC) — read-path, safe to land first; (2) op handler dispatch + execute_op (~800 LOC) — write path; (3) tracked-change / comment subsystem (~400 LOC) — w:ins/w:del + commentsExtended; (4) metadata + render_diff (~300 LOC). Legacy module stays in place additively; Phase-17 (or later) retires the legacy copy after tools/* + tests migrate to the pure-python sibling.

Anchor matches multiple loci; no occurrence index provided
Strict mode + post-pack validation regressed
Could not write .bak
Plan shape rejected at validate_plan step
