DF-001.NAME: Document Generation
DF-001.step-1: M-CREATE selects skill prompt by model tier (M-SKILLS)
DF-001.step-2: Model receives prompt + design tokens → returns JS code or JSON content
DF-001.step-3: Frontier/Medium: M-SANDBOX executes JS → file; Small: M-TEMPLATES fills template → file
DF-001.step-4: M-VALIDATE runs rule checks on produced file
DF-001.step-5: M-FINGERPRINT checks style drift against baseline

DF-002.NAME: Validate and Fix
DF-002.step-1: M-VALIDATE loads rules (M-RULES) and runs against document
DF-002.step-3: If fix requested: M-FIX applies safe+visual fixes, rejects destructive
DF-002.step-4: Backup before any fix; max 3 iterations

DF-003.NAME: Style Extraction
DF-003.step-2: Statistical analysis of layouts and patterns (Layer 2: ~80%)
DF-003.step-3: Output: design-tokens.json for model constraints and validation baseline
DF-003.evidence: [Extract] produced design-tokens.json with colors, typography, layout

DF-004.NAME: QA Structural Analysis
DF-004.step-1: M-QA runs L1 programmatic validation over the OOXML package
DF-004.step-2: M-QA runs L2 structural ZIP analysis over styles, numbering, headers, footers, and document sections
DF-004.step-3: Output: QA report with confidence scores (0-1) and structural findings
DF-004.evidence: [QA][l1][BLOCK_L1_CHECK], [QA][l2][BLOCK_L2_STRUCTURAL], confidence scores in report. Rendered visual overlap detection is deferred until a future Gotenberg-backed VF exists.

DF-005.NAME: GRACE Injection
DF-005.step-2: Generates manifest (structure + fingerprint) and instructions (5-10 rules)
DF-005.step-4: Preserves existing Custom XML from other vendors
DF-005.evidence: [GRACE] manifest + instructions readable via OOXML parsers

DF-006.NAME: Modular Document Generation
DF-006.step-0: M-CREATE checks CreateRequest.modular flag. If True: enter modular path. If False: use existing single-pass path (DF-001).
DF-006.step-1: M-PLAN receives user prompt → LLM generates DocumentPlan JSON. On invalid JSON or failed validation: retry up to max_retries with error context.
DF-006.step-2: M-PLAN validates plan: min 1 section, valid types, standard style IDs, DXA widths, page setup dimensions > 0.
DF-006.step-3: M-SECTION generates sections sequentially: each section receives SiblingRef[] (id+title+type) from already-generated sections. Failed sections retried up to max_retries with refined prompt (original spec + previous error + failed code). Global budget: max_total_retries (default 20).
DF-006.step-4: M-SECTION validates each section code: no imports, no fs, bracket balance, expected component patterns.
DF-006.step-5: Sections that fail all retries: M-SECTION produces placeholder code (title + italic error message). Assembly proceeds with placeholders.
DF-006.step-6: M-ASSEMBLE builds complete JS from template: Document(styles, numbering) + sections mapped by type (cover→no header, toc→TableOfContents, content→normal Section). Section code injected as children array elements.
DF-006.step-7: M-ASSEMBLE executes via M-SANDBOX → output file. Returns AssemblyResult with sections_included, sections_placeholder counts.
DF-006.step-8: M-CREATE postprocesses output (dedup styles, remove empty comments, fix table layout). Postprocessor lives in M-CREATE and is called after both single-pass and modular paths.
DF-006.step-9: M-VALIDATE runs rule checks on final file. CreateResult.execution_mode="modular".

DF-007.NAME: Document Edit Pipeline
DF-007.TRIGGER: mint_edit MCP call or mint edit CLI subcommand
DF-007.step-0: M-EDIT receives document_path + EditPlan JSON (already validated as well-formed by the MCP/CLI shim).
DF-007.step-1: M-EDIT.validate_plan: at least one op, every op_type supported, op_id unique within the plan, anchor.value bounded (≤512 chars, no ASCII control characters), revision ops carry author and date.
DF-007.step-2: M-OOXML.unpack on document_path → unpack_dir; pretty-print, run merge, smart-quote escape, preserve binaries byte-for-byte.
DF-007.step-3: M-EDIT.extract_text_with_anchors over the unpack tree → list[TextAnchor]. (For LLM-driven plans this step also produced the anchors fed to the model in build_edit_prompt; for direct API/CLI plans it is used only by resolve_anchor.)
DF-007.step-4: For each EditOp in plan.ops in order: resolve_anchor against the ORIGINAL tree (raise EDIT_ANCHOR_AMBIGUOUS or EDIT_ANCHOR_NOT_FOUND on mismatch) → execute_op handler mutates the lxml tree in place → record OpOutcome with before/after snippets. Failures stop the pipeline unless an op was marked tolerant (out of scope for v0).
DF-007.step-5: Tracked-change ops route through apply_tracked_change to emit ECMA-376 w:ins/w:del; comment ops update word/comments.xml plus word/commentsExtended.xml (reply linkage via w15:commentEx paraIdParent) plus relationships and content types.
DF-007.step-6: M-OOXML.pack(unpack_dir) → output_path with auto-repair (durableId regeneration, xml:space=preserve insertion, rsid normalization).
DF-007.step-7: M-VALIDATE.run_checks on the packed output (severity from MintConfig). EDIT_VALIDATION_FAILED is surfaced if strict mode and the output regresses.
DF-007.step-8: M-EDIT.render_diff produces the per-op diff list; backup_path is the pre-edit copy. Return EditResult with success flag, ops_total/succeeded/failed, validation_report, diff, duration_ms.
DF-007.evidence: [Edit][validate_plan][BLOCK_EDIT_PLAN_VALIDATE], [Edit][backup][BLOCK_EDIT_BACKUP], [OOXML][unpack][BLOCK_OOXML_UNPACK], [Edit][extract_text][BLOCK_EDIT_EXTRACT_TEXT], [Edit][resolve_anchor][BLOCK_EDIT_RESOLVE_ANCHOR] per op, [Edit][apply_op][BLOCK_EDIT_APPLY_OP] per op (may emit [Edit][tracked_change][BLOCK_EDIT_TRACKED_CHANGE]), [OOXML][pack][BLOCK_OOXML_PACK] (may emit [OOXML][autorepair][BLOCK_OOXML_AUTOREPAIR]), [Validate][run_checks][BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS]

DF-009.NAME: Markdown Source → Document (deterministic, no LLM)
DF-009.TRIGGER: markdown_to_spec(md_text) called from a runner / CLI / MCP tool
DF-009.step-2: markdown-it-py with the gfm-like preset parses md_text → flat token stream + SyntaxTreeNode tree.
DF-009.step-3: Walker iterates the tree top-down. Heading nodes open new SpecSection (level=1..3). Each block-level child node is dispatched: paragraph → ParagraphBlock (with inline emphasis collected into emphasis tuple), table → TableBlock (header from thead, rows from tbody, alignment in attrs), fenced code → CodeBlock(language, content), blockquote → CalloutBlock(kind="info"), bullet/ordered/task list → ListBlock with the matching ListKind. Unknown nodes are folded into the surrounding paragraph as plain text.
DF-009.step-4: Walker assembles ArticleSpec(title, sections, ...). title is title_override if provided, else first H1 text, else fallback "Untitled".
DF-009.step-5: Spec is fed to MP-DOCUMENT.build_document_from_spec via the existing pipeline (MP-STYLE preset, MP-VALIDATE, MP-GRACE if requested).
DF-009.evidence: [MP-MdAdapter][parse][BLOCK_PARSE_MD] with token_count + section_count + block_count_by_type. NO LLM emissions in this DF.

DF-010.NAME: Memo Generation with Elicitation Dialog
DF-010.TRIGGER: mcp.create_memo(intent, source_md?) invoked by an MCP client (Claude Desktop / Cursor / Copilot / OpenWebUI)
DF-010.step-1: MP-MEMO-POC.create_memo receives intent, optional source_md, and FastMCP Context.
DF-010.step-2: If source_md is present: MP-MD-ADAPTER.markdown_to_spec extracts structured content (DF-009). Heuristic field-extraction tries to populate MemoSpec from intent + source: subject from intent's first sentence, recipient/sender if explicitly named, date if date-shaped phrase present, body from source_md content.
DF-010.step-3: required_fields = (sender, recipient, date, subject, body). For each missing or empty field, in declaration order: ctx.elicit(message, response_type) → MCP client renders a structured form to the user → response returns to the tool. If the user rejects (response.rejected) raise MEMO_ELICITATION_REJECTED.
DF-010.step-4: Once all 5 fields present: load templates/memo.yaml (MemoTemplate) → assemble Document via MP-DOCUMENT (header with sender/recipient/date/subject; body section; signature placeholder). Apply klawd preset (MP-STYLE.apply_preset_to_doc).
DF-010.step-5: MP-GRACE.bootstrap on the saved docx with audit metadata: model identity (from ctx if available), timestamp, fields_elicited list, source_md_hash if applicable. Returns audit_id.
DF-010.step-6: Tool returns {"path": str(output_path), "audit_id": audit.id, "fields_elicited": [...]} to the MCP client.
DF-010.evidence: [MP-Memo][create][BLOCK_PARSE_INTENT] with elicitable_fields_initial, [MP-Memo][elicit][BLOCK_ELICIT_FIELD] per missing field with field name + accepted/rejected outcome, [MP-Memo][build][BLOCK_BUILD_DOCX], [MP-Memo][grace][BLOCK_INJECT_GRACE] with audit_id. End-to-end manually verified through Claude Desktop as the first MCP client.

DF-008.NAME: Engine Selection
DF-008.step-1: argparse parses optional top-level `--engine {python,js}`; default None when user omits.
DF-008.step-2: If args.engine is not None, M-CLI.main writes os.environ["MINT_ENGINE"] = args.engine BEFORE the first config() call. This is the override channel — MintConfig stays frozen.
DF-008.step-3: config() lazy-loads MintConfig; load_config reads MINT_ENGINE → Engine enum (default Engine.PYTHON when env unset). Invalid value raises CONFIG_INVALID with a message naming MINT_ENGINE and the valid set {python,js}.
DF-008.step-4: _select_engine(cfg) returns "js" when cfg.engine == Engine.JS; raises NotImplementedError naming Phase 0 + remediation when cfg.engine == Engine.PYTHON.
DF-008.step-5: On "js": legacy cmd_* dispatch proceeds unchanged (DF-001..DF-007 entry conditions unaffected).
DF-008.evidence: [Config][load][BLOCK_LOAD_CONFIG], [CLI][_select_engine][BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE], and [CLI][main][BLOCK_DISPATCH] on the js path. The python path emits BLOCK_LOAD_CONFIG and BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE, then raises NotImplementedError before any cmd_* handler starts.

DF-009.NAME: Pure Python Document Construction
DF-009.step-1: SDK re-export resolves: `from mint_python.sdk import Document, Section, Table, Style` binds public names from MP-DOCUMENT, MP-SECTION, MP-TABLE, MP-STYLE.
DF-009.step-2: Document() constructor applies optional with_style_preset(name) — loads JSON preset from registry; raises STYLE_PRESET_NOT_FOUND if name unknown.
DF-009.step-3: add_cover / add_section / add_toc / set_header / set_footer mutate Document.body in declared order; each returns self for fluent chaining.
DF-009.step-4: save(path) walks body: python-docx Document built; each Section.render() invoked; Paragraph/Table/Image render to python-docx primitives; cover and header/footer rendered via lxml direct write where python-docx lacks API. Emits [MP-Document][save][BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX] at INFO with section_count, table_count, output_path.
DF-009.step-5: Stub methods (inject_grace, validate, fix, to_pdf) emit [MP-Document][stub][BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD] then raise NotImplementedError naming target phase + remediation.
DF-009.evidence: [MP-Document][save][BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX] fires once per save; [MP-Document][stub][BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD] fires when a stub is touched. M-VALIDATE invoked on the saved file (via existing CLI `mint --engine js validate output.docx`) reports zero hard violations on lenient mode for simple text+table documents.

DF-011.NAME: Chart Construction
DF-011.step-1: Factory dispatch: Chart.bar/line/stacked_bar/pie/heatmap/waterfall/gantt accept canonical data shapes; from_matplotlib accepts a pre-built matplotlib.figure.Figure.
DF-011.step-2: Corporate theme application: pull Style namespace from active Document preset (passed via render-time context OR captured at construction via thread-local); apply matplotlib rcParams for font.family, color cycle, axes spine/grid styling (chartjunk removal); fallback to system defaults if Inter font unavailable.
DF-011.step-3: Figure render: matplotlib savefig → BytesIO @ 300 DPI PNG; cached in Chart instance until first render(parent_doc) call.
DF-011.step-4: BLOCK_BUILD_CHART emitted at INFO with chart_type + data_shape (rows × cols for matrix-typed charts; len(values) for 1D).
DF-011.step-5: Plotly stub path: from_plotly emits BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD then raises NotImplementedError naming Phase-9+ before any matplotlib invocation.
DF-011.evidence: [MP-Chart][<factory>][BLOCK_BUILD_CHART] fires once per factory call; [MP-Chart][from_plotly][BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD] fires on stub touch then raises.

DF-012.NAME: Chart Embed in Section
DF-012.TRIGGER: Section.add_chart(chart) called and Section.render(parent_doc) walks the block
DF-012.step-1: Section.add_chart(chart: Chart) appends to _blocks and returns self (post-Phase-8: stub raising BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD is replaced with this concrete implementation).
DF-012.step-2: Section.render iterates _blocks calling block.render(parent_doc) per block; Chart.render is invoked when block is a Chart instance.
DF-012.step-3: Chart.render calls parent_doc.add_picture(BytesIO(self._png_bytes), width=Inches(self.width_inches)); python-docx assigns rId and embeds the image part.
DF-012.step-4: BLOCK_RENDER_CHART emitted at INFO with chart_type + emu_width (resolved EMU value used in the OOXML w:cx attribute).
DF-012.evidence: [MP-Chart][render][BLOCK_RENDER_CHART] fires exactly once per Chart.render call; the chart's inline_shape.width.emu == round(width_inches × 914400) on the saved .docx.

DF-010.NAME: Style Preset Loading
DF-010.step-1: Resolve preset source: registry lookup by name OR JSON file at path.
DF-010.step-2: Load + validate against schema (typography, color_palette, spacing, named_styles dict). On failure raise STYLE_PRESET_INVALID_SCHEMA with field path of the violation.
DF-010.step-3: Materialize Style namespace: heading1, heading2, body, table_header, caption — each a frozen Style instance.
DF-010.step-4: Register on Document for downstream Section/Table/Image to inherit.
DF-010.evidence: [MP-Style][load_preset][BLOCK_LOAD_PRESET] at INFO with preset name + source (registry|file). On failure: structured ValueError with field path.

DF-015.NAME: Pure Python Validation Flow
DF-015.step-1: Document.validate creates a tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile (suffix=.docx, delete=False); copies the in-memory document to the temp path via self.save().
DF-015.step-2: Resolve the level string ("lenient"/"strict"/"audit" or unknown→AUDIT) via _resolve_severity_mode helper; returns SeverityMode enum value.
DF-015.step-3: Call mint_python.validate.run_checks(tmp_path, severity) which opens the saved .docx ZIP, parses word/document.xml + sibling parts via lxml, and evaluates all_rules from the YAML registry.
DF-015.step-4: Classify violations via classify_violations grouping by severity + fix_category; return ValidationReport with totals + mode + passed boolean (depends on level).
DF-015.step-5: finally: tmp_path.unlink(missing_ok=True) — cleanup is unconditional even on exception (VF-016 inv-1 TEMP-FILE-CLEANUP).
DF-015.evidence: [MP-Validate][run_checks][BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS] at INFO with violation count + mode + classification breakdown. NO BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD on this path post-Phase-9 (VF-016 forbidden-1).

DF-016.NAME: Pure Python Auto-Fix Flow
DF-016.step-1: Document.fix saves to tempfile (same pattern as DF-015 step-1).
DF-016.step-2: Call mint_python.fix.fix(tmp_path) which: (a) calls run_checks at LENIENT to get current violations; (b) creates .bak backup via create_backup; (c) iterates safe/visual fixes (D-H09 newline fix is the only Phase-9 implementation); (d) re-validates after each iteration and detects cascade (max 3 iterations).
DF-016.step-3: Destructive violations short-circuit at the start: DestructiveRejectedError raised before any file mutation.
DF-016.step-4: BackupFailedError propagates: backup_path = None then re-raise (no silent swallow).
DF-016.step-5: Cascade detected (no convergence after max iterations): CascadeDetectedError raised with iteration count.
DF-016.step-6: Return FixReport with applied_fixes list, remaining_violations, backup_path, iterations count, diff summary.
DF-016.evidence: [MP-Fix][backup][BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP] + [MP-Fix][apply][BLOCK_APPLY_FIX] at INFO per iteration; ValidationReport (re-validate) emits its own BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS.

DF-017.NAME: Authorized Template Write
DF-017.step-1: update_template receives (name, content, author, ctx). Before any disk I/O it calls MP-AUTH-SHIM.require_template_writer(author).
DF-017.step-2: require_template_writer loads WritersConfig (cached). Sources in priority: MINT_TEMPLATE_WRITERS env, then ~/.config/mint/writers.json. Empty config → open_mode=True (with one-shot BLOCK_AUTH_OPEN_MODE warning per process).
DF-017.step-3: If author NOT in writers AND open_mode is False → raise TEMPLATE_WRITE_FORBIDDEN with author + reason. Tool surfaces structured error; templates/_audit.jsonl gains NO entry; no file written.
DF-017.step-4: If author IS in writers OR open_mode → existing W3 path runs (TemplateRegistry validation → next-semver compute → file write → _audit.jsonl append → GRACE-manifest extension on subsequent create_document calls).
DF-017.evidence: [MP-Auth][check_writer][BLOCK_AUTH_DENY] author=<...> reason=<...> on deny path; [MP-Auth][check_writer][BLOCK_AUTH_OPEN_MODE] once on first open-mode call. The legacy [MP-TemplatesWrite][update][BLOCK_TEMPLATE_WRITE] continues to fire only on the allowed path.

DF-018.NAME: GRACE Manifest Read
DF-018.step-1: mint_read_grace_manifest receives (document_path, ctx). Path normalized via safe_doc — rejects path traversal / absolute traversal patterns with INVALID_DOCUMENT.
DF-018.step-2: MP-MANIFEST-READ delegates to MP-GRACE.describe(path). describe opens the docx zip, locates grace/manifest_*.xml entries, parses XML, returns GRACEManifest dataclass (or None when no GRACE part is present).
DF-018.step-3: None → MANIFEST_NOT_FOUND structured tool error. Parse exception → MANIFEST_PARSE_ERROR. Otherwise canonicalize the dataclass into a dict with keys {audit_id, model_identity, fields_elicited, template, template_version, template_author, timestamp, instructions, fingerprint, namespace} and return.
DF-018.step-4: Multiple manifest_*.xml parts present (W3 append-extension semantics) → return the most recent entry (highest timestamp); earlier entries surfaced under manifest_history if requested via future flag (out of scope for Phase-15).
DF-018.evidence: [MP-Manifest][read][BLOCK_READ_MANIFEST] document_path=<...> manifest_count=<n> on success; [MP-Manifest][read][BLOCK_MANIFEST_NOT_FOUND] when describe returns None.

DF-019.NAME: Visual QA Hook on create_document
DF-019.step-0: create_document completes the existing pipeline: heuristic → elicit → assemble → save → MP-GRACE.bootstrap. Document is on disk and ready to return.
DF-019.step-1: Hook reads MINT_SKIP_VISUAL_QA. If "1" → emit no marker, omit visual_qa key from structured_content, return.
DF-019.step-2: MP-VISUAL-QA-HOOK.score_document(path, preset_name) probes backend availability: soffice on PATH, pdftoppm on PATH, Ollama VLM endpoint reachable. Any missing → log [MP-VisualQA][score][BLOCK_QA_BACKEND_UNAVAILABLE] reason=<...> at WARNING; structured_content gets visual_qa: {skipped: true, skip_reason: ..., advisory: true}; tool succeeds.
DF-019.step-3: All backends present: soffice headless renders docx → PDF; pdftoppm renders first MINT_VISUAL_QA_PAGES pages (default 5) at 110 DPI to PNGs.
DF-019.step-4: For each page: base64 PNG sent to qwen2.5vl:7b with rubric prompt; response is strict JSON with axis scores. Aggregate axis means → overall score.
DF-019.step-5: structured_content gains visual_qa: {score, axes, issues, pages_scored, preset_name, advisory: true}. Below-threshold (default 70) emits [MP-VisualQA][score][BLOCK_QA_BELOW_THRESHOLD] but NEVER raises — tool result still success. Above-threshold emits [MP-VisualQA][score][BLOCK_QA_SCORE].
DF-019.evidence: [MP-VisualQA][score][BLOCK_QA_SCORE] preset=<...> pages_scored=<n> score=<0-100> axes=<dict>; backend-failure variant emits BLOCK_QA_BACKEND_UNAVAILABLE; below-threshold variant emits BLOCK_QA_BELOW_THRESHOLD. Hook position-after-GRACE invariant pins to BLOCK_INJECT_GRACE (document.py:910 marker); BLOCK_INJECT_MANIFEST is the inner MP-GRACE marker that fires earlier (informational, not the integration boundary). Forbidden: any QA failure mode that prevents the docx from being returned to the caller.

DF-020.NAME: Document Hygiene MCP
DF-020.TRIGGER: mint_validate_document / mint_fix_document / mint_fingerprint_document / mint_extract_content MCP call (Phase-16)
DF-020.step-1: Tool receives (document_path, [tool-specific params], ctx). Path normalized via safe_doc — rejects path traversal with INVALID_DOCUMENT before any file I/O.
DF-020.step-2: Tool delegates to its pure-python successor: mint_python.validate.validate / mint_python.fix.fix / mint_python.fingerprint.fingerprint / mint_python.extract.extract_style. None of these import from src/mint/ (constraint-8).
DF-020.step-3: Successor returns dataclass result. Tool canonicalizes to a stable-key-order dict and returns to caller via FastMCP. Mutating tools (fix) preserve their backup-before-write invariant; read tools never open zips in mode 'a' or 'w'.
DF-020.step-4: Errors surface as structured ToolError subclasses with no traceback bleed. Read-path tools (validate / fingerprint / extract) MUST NOT consult MP-AUTH-SHIM (V-MP-AUTH-SHIM forbidden-1 extends). The fix tool also doesn't consult AUTH-SHIM (caller-owned doc, not a template/preset).
DF-020.evidence: [MP-McpValidate][run][BLOCK_VALIDATE_DONE] severity_mode counts; [MP-McpFix][apply][BLOCK_FIX_DONE] iterations applied_count; [MP-McpFingerprint][compute][BLOCK_FP_DONE] hash drift_status; [MP-McpExtract][run][BLOCK_EXTRACT_DONE] format theme_keys layouts_count. Inner-layer markers (BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS, BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP, BLOCK_APPLY_FIX) fire from the underlying mint_python.* successors and are not the integration boundary.

DF-021.NAME: Document Edit via MCP
DF-021.step-1: Tool receives (document_path, plan_json, severity_mode, ctx). safe_doc normalizes path; plan_json is shape-validated via MP-EDIT.validate_plan → EditPlan dataclass.
DF-021.step-2: MP-EDIT.edit runs the existing DF-007 pipeline: backup → unpack → extract_text_with_anchors → for each op (resolve_anchor → execute_op → record OpOutcome) → pack with auto-repair → MP-VALIDATE.run_checks. Backup invariant: a .bak lands beside the original BEFORE any in-place mutation.
DF-021.step-3: Tool canonicalizes EditResult to a dict (per-op {op_id, status, before, after, error?}, validation_report, backup_path, duration_ms) and returns.
DF-021.step-4: Errors surface as structured ToolError with the op_id implicated when anchor resolution fails (helps the caller fix one op at a time). Failure modes preserve the doc's pre-edit state — either the op succeeded and the file is updated, or the .bak is restorable.
DF-021.evidence: [MP-McpEdit][edit][BLOCK_EDIT_DONE] ops_total ops_succeeded ops_failed duration_ms; inner-layer markers (BLOCK_EDIT_BACKUP, BLOCK_EDIT_RESOLVE_ANCHOR per op, BLOCK_EDIT_APPLY_OP per op, BLOCK_OOXML_PACK, BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS) fire from MP-EDIT and remain the audit trail for op-by-op debugging.

DF-022.NAME: Structured Preset Edit
DF-022.step-1: Tool receives (name, <patch>, author, ctx). BEFORE any disk I/O, calls MP-AUTH-SHIM.require_template_writer(author) — reuses MINT_TEMPLATE_WRITERS allowlist (themes are sub-noun of templates per Phase-16 framing).
DF-022.step-2: Tool loads the current preset YAML/JSON (via MP-STYLE.load_preset(name=name); base resolves the BUILTIN_PRESETS entry; later versions resolve `presets/<name>_v<current>.yaml`). Applies the structured patch via _apply_patch (missing patch keys preserve current values; range validation rejects negative spacing, malformed hex, empty font names).
DF-022.step-3: _bump_preset_version computes the next semver (mirrors MP-TEMPLATES-WRITE pattern). Tool writes `presets/<name>_v<next>.yaml` (NEVER overwrites). Sidecar `presets/_audit.jsonl` gains one JSONL entry (timestamp, name, version, author, predecessor_version, patched_fields, content_sha256).
DF-022.step-4: Tool returns {name, version, audit_id, predecessor_version, patched_fields}. The GRACE manifest of any subsequent create_document call that uses this preset embeds the new preset version + author (extending MP-TEMPLATES-WRITE.cross-model-handoff for theme provenance).
DF-022.evidence: [MP-ThemeEdit][<tool>][BLOCK_PRESET_WRITE] name version patched_fields content_sha256; [MP-Auth][check_writer][BLOCK_AUTH_DENY] fires before BLOCK_PRESET_WRITE on the deny path (no write). Cross-module sequence: BLOCK_AUTH_ADMIT (env/file mode) → BLOCK_PRESET_WRITE → presets/_audit.jsonl append.

Phase-1.name: Foundation + Validation (G0+G1)
Phase-1.goal: Prove hypothesis (G0) and build deterministic quality gate (G1)
Phase-1.step-2: Node.js execution sandbox with security constraints
Phase-1.step-3: Validation rule definitions (DOCX hard+soft, PPTX hard)
Phase-1.step-6: StyleFingerprint hash for structural drift detection

Phase-2.name: Claude-Quality Generation (G2)
Phase-2.goal: Any model + MINT ≈ Claude document quality
Phase-2.step-1: Skill prompts per tier and format (docx/pptx × small/medium/frontier)
Phase-2.step-2: Template engine with master templates and JSON fill
Phase-2.step-3: Style extraction: design-tokens.json from existing documents
Phase-2.step-4: Create orchestrator: skill → model → execute → validate → output

Phase-3.name: QA + GRACE + Launch (G3+G4+G5)
Phase-3.goal: Visual QA, GRACE metadata, and public launch
Phase-3.step-1: QA pipeline: L1 programmatic + L2 render-based via Gotenberg
Phase-3.step-2: GRACE injection: manifest + instructions as Custom XML Parts

Phase-4.name: Modular Generation (G6)
Phase-4.goal: Decompose complex documents into sections, generate each independently, assemble deterministically. Target: 90%+ success rate on 35B models for Claude-quality documents.
Phase-4.step-1: Document planner: prompt → structured section plan JSON
Phase-4.step-2: Section generator: plan section → JS code per section
Phase-4.step-3: Document assembler: plan + sections → complete DOCX via sandbox
Phase-4.step-4: Update create orchestrator: add modular mode (plan→section[]→assemble)
Phase-4.step-5: Add MCP tools: mint_plan, mint_generate_section, mint_assemble

Phase-5.name: Edit Pipeline (G7)
Phase-5.goal: Edit existing DOCX/PPTX without regeneration. Model emits a typed JSON EditPlan; runtime applies it deterministically. Unlocks B2B use-cases where users want incremental changes to their own documents.
Phase-5.step-1: Pure-Python pack/unpack utility with pretty-print, run merge, smart-quote escaping, auto-repair
Phase-5.step-2: Edit pipeline: declarative EditPlan → anchor resolve → typed op handlers → pack → validate
Phase-5.step-3: Add MCP tool mint_edit (input document_path + EditPlan JSON, output EditResult); also extend src/mint/cli.py with a mint edit subcommand reading EditPlan from a JSON file

Phase-6.name: Pure Python Edition — Phase 0 (skeleton + feature flag)
Phase-6.goal: Lay groundwork for the Pure Python Edition: parallel `src/mint_python/` skeleton (no engine code) and a dual-engine feature flag (MINT_ENGINE / --engine) wired through M-CONFIG and M-CLI. Default engine=python — picking it raises NotImplementedError from a single chokepoint in M-CLI._select_engine; engine=js keeps the legacy pipeline working unchanged. Phases 1-5 of the pure-python rollout populate MP-* subpackages and replace the chokepoint with a real router.
Phase-6.step-1: Extend MintConfig with `engine: Engine` field (default Engine.PYTHON); add Engine StrEnum {JS,PYTHON}; parse MINT_ENGINE in load_config mirroring the tier/severity pattern (raises CONFIG_INVALID on unknown values).
Phase-6.step-2: Add top-level `--engine {python,js}` arg in build_parser (default None to distinguish "unset" from explicit "js"); in main(), if args.engine is set, write os.environ["MINT_ENGINE"] before any config() call; add private _select_engine chokepoint called once before the cmd_* dispatch dict. _select_engine MUST emit `[CLI][_select_engine][BLOCK_SELECT_ENGINE]` at INFO with the resolved engine value (Phase-6 trace contract; required by VF-011, V-MP-FLAG scenarios 9-10, and V-M-CLI scenario 13).
Phase-6.step-3: Create `src/mint_python/{__init__.py,core/__init__.py,sdk/__init__.py,execution/__init__.py,rules/__init__.py,grace/__init__.py,mcp/__init__.py,cli/__init__.py,tests/__init__.py}` using the repo's `# FILE:` / `# START_MODULE_CONTRACT` header convention. Add `__version__='0.0.0'` to top-level marker. No public interfaces.
Phase-6.step-4: Update pyproject.toml `[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel].packages = ["src/mint", "src/mint_python"]`; bump src/mint/__init__.py to 0.2.0; add `# MINT_ENGINE=js` opt-in line to `.env.example`; pin `tests/integration/test_cli.py::_run` helper to pass `--engine js` so the legacy integration suite stays green under the new python-default.
Phase-6.step-5: Add `tests/unit/test_engine_flag.py`: default→python, env override→js, garbage→CONFIG_INVALID, _select_engine js/python branches, CLI overrides env (via os.environ pre-load), import smoke for all 9 mint_python entry points.

Phase-7.name: Pure Python Edition — Phase 1 (Core Document + SDK per handover §3, §6)
Phase-7.goal: Land the foundational Pure Python document construction stack (handover §3.1, §3.2, §3.3, §3.5). Concrete: Style + Pt + ColorPalette + presets; Paragraph + Run + Image; Table with §3.3 factories; Section fluent; Document with save() producing a valid .docx via python-docx + lxml; SDK re-exports. Stubs (NotImplementedError naming target phase): Document.{inject_grace, validate, fix, to_pdf}, Section.add_chart. Phase-7 closes with VF-013 e2e green: a 3-section golden document opens in Word and passes M-VALIDATE on lenient mode. Phase-1 of pure-python rollout per handover §6.
Phase-7.step-1: Style frozen dataclass + Pt(value) helper + ColorPalette resolver + load_preset(name|path) returning Style namespace. Built-in registry: 'alga_corporate', 'minimal', 'compact'. JSON schema for presets documented under docs/style-preset-schema.md. Emits [MP-Style][load_preset][BLOCK_LOAD_PRESET].
Phase-7.step-2: Paragraph.add_run(text, style=None) fluent; Run as styled fragment; Image.from_path / Image.from_bytes embedding via python-docx + pillow. Each renders into a python-docx Document. Emits [MP-Content][render][BLOCK_RENDER_CONTENT] when serialized.
Phase-7.step-3: Table model + factories per §3.3 (from_list, from_markdown, from_list_of_dicts, financial, comparison) + apply_style + set_column_widths + autofit. Renders into python-docx Table via .render(parent_doc). Emits [MP-Table][render][BLOCK_RENDER_TABLE].
Phase-7.step-4: Section(title, level).add_paragraph/.add_table/.add_image fluent chain. add_chart raises NotImplementedError naming Phase 2 (matplotlib chart impl). Emits [MP-Section][add_chart][BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD] in the stub path. NOTE: Phase-8/step-2 (commit caf30ab) unstubbed Section.add_chart — current behavior is concrete `add_chart(chart: Chart) -> Section` appending to _blocks. This Phase-7 description is preserved as historical journal; see Phase-8/step-2 for the current contract.
Phase-7.step-5: Document facade with full §3.1 API surface; save() implemented via python-docx + lxml direct-write for cover/header/footer; stubs (inject_grace/validate/fix/to_pdf) all emit BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD then raise NotImplementedError naming target phase. Emits [MP-Document][save][BLOCK_SAVE_DOCX] with section_count, table_count, output_path.
Phase-7.step-6: src/mint_python/sdk/__init__.py re-exports Document, Section, Table, Style, Image, TOC, Pt, ColorPalette + presets registry. with_style_from(path) loader. NO public exports beyond what handover §3 names. NOTE: Phase-8/step-3 (commit caf30ab) added Chart to the SDK __all__ list per handover §3.4 — current public surface is 9 §3 types + presets. This Phase-7 description is preserved as historical journal; see Phase-8/step-3 for the current export list.
Phase-7.step-7: Update pyproject.toml [project].dependencies + uv.lock with python-docx ≥1.1 and pillow ≥10.x (landed mid-Wave-7-2 by the MP-CONTENT worker who hit the missing-deps gap; technology.xml already declared them in the Phase-7 plan commit). Note: docs/style-preset-schema.md was provisioned earlier by $grace-verification (commit 8163785) ahead of Wave-7-1 dispatch — not produced during Phase-7 implementation; treat its presence as an upstream verification artifact.
Phase-7.step-8: Add tests/integration/test_mp_e2e.py: build a 3-section golden document (cover + 2 body sections, each with a paragraph + a table) via mint_python.sdk, save to tmp, run existing M-VALIDATE on lenient mode, assert zero hard violations + non-empty soft list (audit baseline). Reused by future phases as the python-engine regression anchor.

Phase-8.name: Pure Python Edition — Phase 2 (Chart + matplotlib per handover §3.4, §6)
Phase-8.goal: Land MP-CHART unblocking Section.add_chart. Concrete: 7 chart factories (bar, line, stacked_bar, pie, heatmap, waterfall, gantt) + from_matplotlib (primary user channel) + from_seaborn (lazy-import alias) on matplotlib backend; PNG @ 300 DPI rendering with corporate theme application + chartjunk removal; vector kwarg accepted for forward-compat but Phase-8 ships PNG only. Stubs (NotImplementedError + BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD): Chart.from_plotly. In-place updates retire two BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD emit sites: Section.add_chart (becomes concrete) and MP-SDK Chart re-export (becomes available). Phase-8 closes with VF-014: a chart-bearing 2-section document passes M-VALIDATE on lenient mode AND chart inline_shape EMU widths match requested. VF-013 (Phase-7 chart-free golden doc) MUST stay green — non-regression guard. Phase-2 of pure-python rollout per handover §6.
Phase-8.step-1: Chart @dataclass with cached PNG bytes + width/height/caption + chart_type tag. 7 factory classmethods (bar/line/stacked_bar/pie/heatmap/waterfall/gantt) build matplotlib Figure with corporate theme applied (rcParams from active Style preset; fallback graceful), savefig to BytesIO @ 300 DPI PNG. from_matplotlib(fig, caption, width_inches, vector=True) wraps an externally-built Figure; vector kwarg accepted but Phase-8 ships PNG. from_seaborn lazy-imports seaborn (raises ImportError with "pip install seaborn" hint if missing) then aliases to from_matplotlib. from_plotly STUB emits [MP-Chart][from_plotly][BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD] then raises NotImplementedError naming Phase-9+. Each factory emits [MP-Chart][<factory>][BLOCK_BUILD_CHART] chart_type=<name> data_shape=<...> at INFO. .render(parent_doc) calls parent_doc.add_picture with PNG bytes + Inches(width); emits [MP-Chart][render][BLOCK_RENDER_CHART] chart_type=<name> emu_width=<int>. Chartjunk removal: top + right spines off, gridlines styled per Style preset.
Phase-8.step-2: In-place update: replace Section.add_chart stub (currently raises NotImplementedError + emits BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD) with concrete `add_chart(chart: Chart) -> Section` that appends to _blocks and returns self. Section.render unchanged — Chart.render protocol matches Paragraph/Table/Image siblings. Retire V-MP-SECTION scenario-5 (NotImplementedError) + scenario-6 (BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD on add_chart) — replace with positive scenarios verifying chart appended and Section.render walks Chart.render.
Phase-8.step-3: In-place update: add Chart to the public re-export list per handover §3 surface. Update __all__; bump CHANGE_SUMMARY to note Phase-8 surface addition. V-MP-SDK scenario-1 (§3 import list) extends with Chart.
Phase-8.step-4: DOCUMENTED EXCEPTION: matplotlib >=3.7,<4 is pre-installed by the controller in pre-Wave-8-1 controller setup (verification-plan.xml#SubagentExecutionPackets/pre-wave-8-1-controller-checklist/item-1). Rationale: chart.py would ImportError at module load on the Wave-8-1 worker's test runner without matplotlib in the env; pre-installing avoids the Phase-7 Wave-7-2-style mid-wave deviation pattern where worker B had to add python-docx + pillow to pyproject as an inevitable in-flight scope expansion. This is the ONLY Phase-8 step where the "deps follow code" hygiene rule is intentionally relaxed — matplotlib is a known dep with no surprise factor. Step-4 itself, when it runs in Wave-8-2, covers (a) docs/technology.xml: matplotlib moves from future-dep to active dep (already done in commit c7e38a5); (b) seaborn documented as optional (NOT added to pyproject — lazy-imported in Chart.from_seaborn); (c) uv.lock refresh after MP-CHART code lands (the lockfile changes naturally as the dep-graph resolves around the new chart.py module).
Phase-8.step-5: Add tests/integration/test_mp_chart_e2e.py: build a 2-section chart-bearing document (each section: 1 paragraph + 1 chart of different type — e.g. bar + line) via mint_python.sdk; save → reopen → assert chart count + each inline_shape.width.emu matches requested + M-VALIDATE lenient hard_count=0. Pin structural fingerprint to tests/fixtures/mp_chart_e2e_baseline.json with MP_CHART_E2E_WRITE_BASELINE=1 gate (mirrors VF-013 protocol). Re-run VF-013 e2e to confirm chart-free golden doc still green (non-regression).

Phase-9.name: Pure Python Edition — Phase 3 (Validation + Fix + PostProcessor per handover §6)
Phase-9.goal: Land MP-RULES, MP-VALIDATE, MP-FIX as pure-python successors to M-RULES/M-VALIDATE/M-FIX. Unstub Document.validate(level) and Document.fix(strategy). Gate-Phase-9: 100% coverage, ruff/mypy clean, VF-013+VF-014 non-regression green.
Phase-9.step-1: Rule/Violation dataclasses, Severity/FixCategory enums, YAML loader (shared rules/*.yaml), XPath evaluate via lxml, all_rules registry. 4 check types: exists, count_gt_zero, tbl_width_mismatch, sum_mismatch. Emits BLOCK_LOAD_RULES + BLOCK_EVALUATE_RULE.
Phase-9.step-2: run_checks engine: open .docx/.pptx zip, parse XML via lxml, evaluate all_rules, classify violations, return ValidationReport. SeverityMode enum (AUDIT/LENIENT/STRICT). Invalid documents return XML-001 report (never raise at top level). Emits BLOCK_RUN_CHECKS.
Phase-9.step-3: Auto-fix engine: backup → destructive filter → iterate safe/visual D-H09 fix → re-validate → cascade detection (max 3 iterations). D-H09 newline fix only in Phase-9 scope. Emits BLOCK_CREATE_BACKUP + BLOCK_APPLY_FIX.
Phase-9.step-4: Unstub Document.validate(level) → temp-file delegation to MP-VALIDATE.run_checks → returns ValidationReport. Unstub Document.fix(strategy) → temp-file delegation to MP-FIX.fix → returns FixReport. inject_grace and to_pdf remain stubs.
Phase-9.step-5: Add tests/integration/test_mp_validate_fix_e2e.py: validate pipeline (Document.validate→report), fix pipeline (Document.fix→report), temp-file cleanup, BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD absence on validate/fix. VF-013+VF-014 non-regression green.

Phase-10.name: Pure Python Edition — Phase 4 (Execution tiers + RestrictedPython sandbox per handover §6)
Phase-10.SKIP_RATIONALE: Roadmap pivot 2026-05-09: Phase-11 (GRACE + Gotenberg) prioritized over execution-tier sandbox. MP-EXEC-{SMALL,MEDIUM,FRONTIER} + RestrictedPython sandbox + VF-012 PurePythonRegression activation deferred to a future phase TBD. M-SANDBOX (legacy js engine) remains the only execution path until the skip is unwound — VF-012 deprecation-trigger remains unsatisfied. Decision recorded for traceability; see SESSION_HANDOFF_2026-05-09.md.
Phase-10.note: Skipped by design — not abandoned. The handover §6 Phase 4 scope (sandboxed Python execution for tier=frontier code) remains in the roadmap but is sequenced after Phase-11 GRACE/PDF work.

Phase-11.name: Pure Python Edition — Phase 5 (GRACE + Gotenberg PDF per handover §3.1, §6)
Phase-11.goal: Land MP-GRACE as a pure-python successor to M-GRACE. Unstub Document.inject_grace and Document.to_pdf — the last two stubs declared in MP-DOCUMENT MODULE_MAP that retire surfaces deferred from Phase-7. Gate-Phase-11: 100% coverage maintained, ruff/mypy clean, e2e tests for GRACE injection + PDF generation green.
Phase-11.step-1: MP-GRACE module: GRACEManifest dataclass + bootstrap (inject manifest as Custom XML Part) + describe (read back existing manifest). 10 default instruction rules (GRACE_INSTRUCTIONS). Path-traversal guard on source ZIP. Stdlib-only deps (zipfile, xml.etree, hashlib, uuid, tempfile). Emits BLOCK_INJECT_MANIFEST + BLOCK_PRESERVE_EXISTING.
Phase-11.step-2: In-place unstub: Document.inject_grace → temp-file delegation to MP-GRACE.bootstrap. Phase-7 BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD on inject_grace retired.
Phase-11.step-3: In-place unstub: Document.to_pdf → Gotenberg HTTP adapter (POST /forms/libreoffice/convert with the saved docx; receive PDF bytes; write to caller-supplied path). Phase-7 BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD on to_pdf retired.
Phase-11.step-4: Add tests/unit/test_mp_grace.py + tests/integration/test_mp_grace_e2e.py + tests/integration/test_mp_pdf_e2e.py covering: bootstrap idempotency under fingerprint, describe round-trip, vendor Custom XML Parts preservation (non-GRACE customXml entries unchanged after injection), Gotenberg adapter happy path + error path. VF-013 + VF-014 + VF-015 + VF-016 all stay green (non-regression).

Phase-12.name: Pure Python Edition — Phase 6 (Chart.from_plotly + kaleido per handover §3.4)
Phase-12.goal: Retire the last MP-CHART stub: Chart.from_plotly. Pure Python Edition reaches ZERO active stubs across the entire SDK surface — Document.{validate, fix, inject_grace, to_pdf} + Section.add_chart + Chart.from_plotly all concrete. The from_plotly path piggybacks on existing DF-011 Chart Construction (no new DF needed); plotly + kaleido remain optional deps via lazy-import pattern (mirrors seaborn precedent from Phase-8).
Phase-12.step-1: In-place unstub: replace from_plotly stub raising NotImplementedError with concrete delegation to plotly.io.to_image (scale=2 PNG via kaleido backend). Lazy-import contract preserved — plotly + kaleido remain optional deps; missing-dep error path raises ImportError with installation hint, NOT BLOCK_PHASE_GUARD. Chart instance carries chart_type="plotly" tag in trace payloads.
Phase-12.step-2: Update tests/unit/test_mp_chart.py: retire scenario-10 NotImplementedError assertion; add positive Plotly Figure → Chart construction tests + missing-dep ImportError tests (plotly absent + plotly-OK-kaleido-absent both covered). +2 net tests; coverage 100% maintained.
Phase-12.step-3: Document plotly + kaleido as optional deps in docs/technology.xml mirroring seaborn precedent. NOT added to pyproject.toml [project].dependencies — lazy-import pattern keeps the wheel slim; users opting into Plotly install via `pip install plotly kaleido` (or via a [project.optional-dependencies] charts-plotly extras group, future cleanup).

Phase-13.name: MCP-first product shape — input adapters, MCP audit, Memo POC with elicitation
Phase-13.goal: Reposition MINT toward MCP-first product shape per session 2026-05-10. Three modules in declared order: MP-MD-ADAPTER (deterministic markdown → ArticleSpec walker, fixes the embarrassing LLM-flattened baseline_report.docx) → MP-MCP-AUDIT (design-only review of legacy mcp_g1 + mcp_g2, becomes brief for Phase-14 migration) → MP-MEMO-POC (FastMCP create_memo tool with ctx.elicit() planning dialog, Memo template as YAML data file, klawd-themed render, GRACE audit-trail). Establishes the differentiating bundle (design tokens + governed templates + GRACE audit + elicitation-driven dialogs) vs flat-tool docx-MCP servers (GongRzhe/Office-Word-MCP-Server), format converters (mcp-pandoc), and Claude-only Anthropic Skills. End state: the alpha-baseline can be rerun deterministically from markdown source (no LLM); a reference Memo doc-type is end-to-end demoable through Claude Desktop with planning dialog.
Phase-13.step-1: Add markdown-it-py 4.2 to pyproject.toml. Implement src/mint_python/adapters/markdown.py — markdown_to_spec(md_text, *, title_override=None) → ArticleSpec via SyntaxTreeNode walker. Cover scenarios 1-10 of V-MP-MD-ADAPTER (heading depths, GFM tables with alignment, fenced code, blockquotes → callouts, bullet/numbered/task lists, inline emphasis, malformed input graceful handling, round-trip md→spec→docx, REPORT_COMPARISON.md regenerated as a respectable docx, empty/null input). 0 LLM calls in production path. Quick win — unblocks the embarrassing baseline_report and validates the input-adapter pattern as canonical.
Phase-13.step-2: Read src/mint/mcp_g1.py + src/mint/mcp_g2.py + src/mint/llm.py. Produce docs/mcp-audit.md: (a) inventory all @mcp.tool registrations with current signatures and behavior; (b) classify each tool stateful/stateless, elicitation-aware/one-shot, kept/migrate/deprecate; (c) gap analysis vs the new product shape — design-tokens-as-resources (currently absent), versioned templates (absent), GRACE-as-audit-trail (partially shipped), elicitation-driven planning (absent); (d) migration plan with priorities; (e) roadmap of new tools required for Phase-14 (list_templates, get_template, update_template, list_presets, generate_document with elicitation). No code in this step — design only. Phase-14 will execute the migration based on this brief.
Phase-13.step-3: Define MemoSpec frozen dataclass with 5 required fields (sender, recipient, date, subject, body) + MemoTemplate loader for templates/memo.yaml (declares field order, layout, klawd-derived refinements). Implement @mcp.tool create_memo in src/mint_python/mcp/memo.py: parse intent + optional source_md via MP-MD-ADAPTER → populate partial MemoSpec heuristically → ctx.elicit() per missing required field in declaration order → assemble Document with klawd preset → MP-GRACE.bootstrap with audit metadata → return path + audit_id + fields_elicited. Cover V-MP-MEMO-POC scenarios 1-8 (full intent / partial intent / multi-round elicit / rejection / lenient validation / klawd visual / GRACE injection / log marker presence). Manual smoke through Claude Desktop as the first MCP client. OpenWebUI compat is a follow-up verification, not new dev work in this phase.

Phase-14.name: Generic doc-gen + governed templates + MCP resources (per docs/mcp-audit.md)
Phase-14.goal: Lift Phase-13's MEMO-POC out of its hardcoded "memo specifically" shape into the production product surface promised by docs/mcp-audit.md. Four independent waves shipping the differentiating bundle (governed templates + MCP resources + generic doc-gen + GRACE-as-audit-trail) end-to-end. After Phase-14, adding a new document type (Letter, Report, Contract, Invoice) is a YAML file in templates/ — zero Python required. The MCP server becomes the governance layer the audit described: templates and presets live as versioned MCP resources; a frontier model can edit a template skeleton in low-sensitivity context, a local model later consumes it on sensitive client data, GRACE manifests embed the version + author chain into every produced document. This phase is what finally lets us claim "MCP-first product shape" honestly — Phase-13 was the POC that proved the elicitation + chat-driven-fallback pipeline works on real Claude Desktop; Phase-14 productionizes it.
Phase-14.step-1: **W1 — generic create_document tool**. Refactor `src/mint_python/mcp/memo.py` into `src/mint_python/mcp/document.py` exposing `create_document(intent, doc_type, source_md?, ctx) -> ToolResult`. Pipeline becomes type-agnostic: lookup `templates/<doc_type>.yaml`, run the same heuristic+elicit+build+grace flow Memo-POC validated. The hardcoded MemoSpec dataclass is replaced by a generic schema generated from the template's `required_fields` declaration; the `_heuristic_extract` function takes a list of field names rather than fixed attributes. Memo continues to work via `doc_type="memo"`; new types are a YAML file each. Existing `create_memo` tool stays as a thin alias (`create_memo(intent) → create_document(intent, doc_type="memo")`) for backwards compatibility on existing Claude Desktop sessions. Audit's Priority-3 (elicitation migration) effectively lands here too — elicit + chat-driven-fallback contract inherited verbatim. ~50-80 LOC net (mostly extracts; some fields-driven generalization).
Phase-14.step-2: **W2 — template registry + read tools**. New module `src/mint_python/templates/registry.py`. Walks `templates/` for `<name>.yaml` (and optionally `<name>_v<semver>.yaml`); validates against the generic TemplateSchema (extends MemoTemplate). New MCP tools `list_templates(ctx) -> list[TemplateSummary]` (name, version, doc_type, description, last_modified) and `get_template(name, version="latest", ctx) -> TemplateContent` (full YAML content as a dict). Implements audit's Priority-2 partially — template side; preset side is W4. Adds a new doc-type: `templates/letter.yaml` to demonstrate the registry doesn't depend on the memo special-case (Letter has different fields: sender_address, recipient_address, salutation, body, closing).
Phase-14.step-3: **W3 — template versioning + cross-model handoff**. New tool `update_template(name, content, author, ctx) -> {name, version, audit_id, predecessor_version}`. Writes `templates/<name>_v<next-semver>.yaml`; never overwrites existing files. Sidecar audit log `templates/_audit.jsonl` records every write with timestamp, author, predecessor version, content hash. GRACE manifest in produced docx now records the template version + author lineage (extending the existing _audit_instructions list). Demo path (manual): frontier Claude edits memo_v1.0 → memo_v1.1 in low-sensitivity context (no client data); local qwen3.5 uses memo_v1.1 to generate a memo with sensitive client data; produced docx's GRACE manifest names the template version Claude authored. Implements audit's Priority-4. The cross-model demo is the closest thing we have to a "wow moment" against ChatGPT/Anthropic-Skills competitors that don't do governance.
Phase-14.step-4: **W4 — MCP resources for templates + presets**. Wire FastMCP `@server.resource` handlers exposing `mint://template/<name>[/v<version>]` and `mint://preset/<name>` URI schemes. Clients that prefer `resources/list` + `resources/read` over tool-calls (Cursor, VS Code with Insiders MCP) get native enumeration without making a tool call per template. Symmetric tools `list_presets(ctx)` and `get_preset(name, ctx)` for clients that don't support the resources protocol (current Claude Desktop). Implements audit's Priority-1 (design-tokens-as-resources). After this wave, the MCP-audit.md document's gap-analysis section can flip all four differentiators from "absent" to "shipped".

Phase-15.name: Production hardening (per HANDOVER-phase15-ready.md)
Phase-15.goal: "можно показать клиенту O!Bank, не страшно". Phase-14 made MINT MCP-first; Phase-15 makes it safe to demo externally. Three production-grade modules + one cleanup wave: (a) gate write-path tools behind a config-driven allowlist so a stray sidecar can't clobber governed templates; (b) ship the read counterpart to GRACE injection so callers can audit a docx they didn't generate (closes audit Priority-1 acceptance); (c) attach an advisory VLM rubric scorer to create_document so demo runs surface visual regressions before they reach a customer screen; (d) finally remove the legacy mcp_g1/mcp_g2 surface and the JS-sandbox path the Phase-14 audit promised to delete but didn't. After Phase-15 the public surface is pure-python, every write is authorized, every produced docx is auditable + visually scored, and the legacy Node tail is gone.
Phase-15.step-1: **W1 — Authorization shim (production blocker)**. New module src/mint_python/mcp/auth.py: WritersConfig dataclass, load_writers_config (env + ~/.config/mint/writers.json, cached, AUTH_CONFIG_INVALID on malformed), is_template_writer, require_template_writer (raises TEMPLATE_WRITE_FORBIDDEN). Wire MP-TEMPLATES-WRITE.update_template to call require_template_writer BEFORE any disk mutation; the existing audit-log entry path is unchanged. Empty config = open mode with one-shot BLOCK_AUTH_OPEN_MODE warning per process — backwards-compat for local dev. ~80 LOC + tests + a 10-line config docs section in templates/_README.md.
Phase-15.step-2: **W1 — GRACE manifest read tool**. New module src/mint_python/mcp/manifest.py exposing @server.tool async mint_read_grace_manifest(document_path, ctx). Thin wrapper over the existing MP-GRACE.describe — opens docx zip via safe_doc, returns canonical dict (audit_id, model_identity, fields_elicited, template, template_version, template_author, timestamp, instructions, fingerprint, namespace) or surfaces MANIFEST_NOT_FOUND / MANIFEST_PARSE_ERROR / INVALID_DOCUMENT structured errors. ~50 LOC + tests. W1-parallel-safe with MP-AUTH-SHIM (independent files; no shared write scope).
Phase-15.step-3: **W2 — Visual QA hook**. Refactor root-level e2e_qa_visual.py into src/mint_python/qa/visual.py as a library: score_document(path, preset_name='klawd') -> VisualQAReport | None. Backend probes (soffice/pdftoppm/Ollama) degrade gracefully — backend-unavailable returns None with a logged warning, tool still succeeds. Hook wired into mcp/document.py post-GRACE step; structured_content gains visual_qa key (or is omitted entirely when MINT_SKIP_VISUAL_QA=1). VLM rubric (qwen2.5vl:7b on Ollama, 0-100 per axis: typography / layout / visual_richness / professional). Default ON; advisory only — visual-QA outcome NEVER blocks document return. ~100 LOC + tests; existing root-level e2e_qa_visual.py becomes a 5-line CLI shim or is retired.
Phase-15.step-4: **W3 — Legacy deprecation (subtractive, EXPANDED scope)**. Pre-flight 2026-05-10 found mint.sandbox imported by mint.create + mint.assemble; mcp_g1/mcp_g2 mounted by mint.cli::cmd_serve. Scope expanded by user approval same day. Delete: src/mint/mcp_g1.py, src/mint/mcp_g2.py, src/mint/sandbox/, src/mint/create.py (legacy M-CREATE; ~120 LOC; superseded by mint_python.mcp.document.create_document since Phase-14), src/mint/assemble.py (legacy M-ASSEMBLE; ~700 LOC; only consumer was mint.create). Edit src/mint/cli.py: drop cmd_serve subcommand entirely (legacy MCP server mount path; the MCP entry point is now src/mint_python/mcp/document.py invoked via stdio); drop Engine.JS branch from _select_engine; remove --engine js arg. Drop Engine.JS enum from src/mint/config.py. Purge docx (9.x) / pptxgenjs (3.x) / exceljs (4.x) / vm2 / isolated-vm / vitest / eslint + any project entry points referencing deleted modules from pyproject.toml + uv.lock. Update docs/technology.xml Runtime + Dependencies + Tooling sections + docs/requirements.xml constraint-7. Delete tests/integration/test_mcp_g1.py + tests/integration/test_mcp_g2.py + tests/unit/test_sandbox.py + tests/unit/test_create.py + tests/unit/test_assemble.py + tests/integration/test_create*.py + tests/integration/test_assemble*.py (all that target deleted modules); tests/integration/test_cli.py loses --engine js + cmd_serve branch coverage. Add tests/unit/test_legacy_deprecation.py asserting ModuleNotFoundError for ALL deleted modules + dep absence. tools/article_experiment/ + scripts/ pre-flight CLEAN — no migration needed there. ~1000 LOC removal + test migration + docs sweep. W3 runs LAST — broad subtractive blast radius; SWARM-FORBIDDEN by V-MP-LEGACY-DEPRECATION autonomy-assessment.

Phase-16.name: MCP-tool parity + new doc_types + structured preset editor (per HANDOVER-phase16-ready.md)
Phase-16.goal: Close the remaining MCP-surface gap from Phase-15 and ship the differentiating bundle for O!Bank demo: validate / fix / fingerprint / extract / edit all reachable through governed `mint_*` MCP tools (track A); four new doc_types — report / ADR / contract / bilingual Ru-En NDA — land as YAML-only additions to the existing generic pipeline (track B); structured preset-edit tools let a model adjust theme tokens (palette / typography / spacing) without composing YAML by hand (track C). End state: the entire post-Phase-15 legacy `src/mint/{validate,fix,fingerprint,extract,edit}.py` surface has pure-python siblings under `src/mint_python/`; every read+write path is reachable through a `mint_*` MCP tool; preset edits run through the same allowlist that gates template edits; four new doc_types extend the demo catalog without Python changes. Phase-16 honors the Phase-15 immediate next action by rolling the `0.4.0a4 → 0.4.0a5` bump into the closeout commit (v0.4.0a4 was tagged 2026-05-11 on the bump commit 2e389c2 — Phase-15 shipped).
Phase-16.step-1: **Wave-16-1** — Thin MCP wrap over mint_python.validate. Read-only; no allowlist (V-MP-AUTH-SHIM forbidden-1 extends). Parallel-safe with the rest of W1 (disjoint write scopes — own file under src/mint_python/mcp/). Tail-import registered in src/mint_python/mcp/document.py during W1 controller integration.
Phase-16.step-2: **Wave-16-1** — Thin MCP wrap calling mint_python.fix.run_checks + mint_python.fix.apply_fixes directly (NOT the high-level fix() helper) so the max_iterations contract parameter is honored. Mutates caller-supplied path; backup-before-write invariant preserved. No allowlist (caller-owned doc). Tail-import registered during W1 controller integration.
Phase-16.step-3: **Wave-16-1** — Ported src/mint/fingerprint.py to src/mint_python/fingerprint.py preserving the public symbol names. RESHAPED FingerprintResult to dev-plan-spec fields {hash, format, has_styles_xml, byte_count} (legacy had {hash, document_path, format, xml_sources}); renamed DriftStatus.BASELINE_MISSING → UNKNOWN. Hash output byte-identical to legacy (porting-parity verified). Legacy module stays in place additively.
Phase-16.step-4: **Wave-16-1** — Ported src/mint/extract.py to src/mint_python/extract.py (extract_style / parse_theme / analyze_layouts + ExtractionFailedError / UnsupportedFormatError). Flat dict output shape preserved per forbidden-3 NO-DIVERGENCE. NAMESPACES + _detect_format duplicated inline (constraint-8). V-MP-EXTRACT scenario-4 reinterpreted: legacy parity requires missing-theme to return empty dict (NOT raise) — corrupt-zip / missing-file paths raise ExtractionFailedError.
Phase-16.step-5: **Wave-16-1** — Four YAML doc_types under templates/. Zero Python; consumed by existing MP-DOC-GENERIC pipeline. Bilingual NDA uses stacked Ru/En layout (paired <field>_<lang> required_fields + adjacent paragraph blocks — canonical recipe reusable for future i18n doc_types). V-MP-DOC-BUNDLE scenario-7 split: 7a (template name+version in GRACE manifest — DONE); 7b (language metadata in _audit_instructions — DEFERRED, requires document.py change out of bundle scope).
Phase-16.step-6: **Wave-16-2** — MCP wrap over MP-FINGERPRINT. Canonicalizes the W1-reshaped FingerprintResult into a 5-key dict; drift_status is JSON null when no baseline_hash provided (wrap short-circuits compare()). Tail-import registered in document.py during W2 controller integration.
Phase-16.step-7: **Wave-16-2** — MCP wrap over MP-EXTRACT. Chose option (B) nested reshape: returns 4-key dict {format, theme:{colors,typography,xml_sources}, layouts, extracted_at} — the W1 port's flat dict is the porting-parity oracle (forbidden-3 bound), but the MCP wrap is a fresh public surface so the nested shape from dev-plan ships. Missing-theme docx returns success with empty theme (legacy parity inherited). Tail-import registered during W2 controller integration.
Phase-16.step-8: **Wave-16-2** — Three structured-patch MCP tools (palette/typography/spacing). Gates through existing MP-AUTH-SHIM (MINT_TEMPLATE_WRITERS allowlist reused per VF-022 inv-2; NO separate MINT_PRESET_WRITERS). Versioned preset YAML via O_EXCL atomic write (concurrent-write race deterministic); sidecar presets/_audit.jsonl. V-MP-THEME-EDIT scenario-10 DEFERRED — document.py doesn't stamp preset_version into GRACE manifest yet; shared root cause with V-MP-DOC-BUNDLE scenario-7b. KNOWN GAP: MP-MCP-RESOURCES mint://preset/<name> handler reads BUILTIN_PRESETS only and does NOT chain to versioned files; after a preset edit, the new version is NOT discoverable via mint:// URIs (Phase-17 candidate).
Phase-16.step-9: **Wave-16-3** — Port src/mint/edit.py (~2003 LOC) to src/mint_python/edit.py preserving the legacy public surface. Largest single port of the Pure Python Edition cycle. Controller pre-flight: brief the worker section-by-section (anchor resolver / op apply / tracked-change / metadata); pre-decide whether to bring src/mint/ooxml.py along as a scope-expansion MP-OOXML port. SWARM-CAREFUL — single owner.
Phase-16.step-10: **Wave-16-3** — MCP wrap over MP-EDIT. Depends on step-9. Mutates caller-supplied path; backup invariant inherited. No allowlist (caller-owned doc).
Phase-16.step-gate: **Wave-16-4** — Gate-Phase-16 + version bump 0.4.0a4 → 0.4.0a5 + handover archive. Full test suite green + ruff/mypy/build clean + grep gate (V-MP-EDIT et al inv-1 NO-LEGACY-IMPORT: zero hits for `from mint\.\(fingerprint\|extract\|edit\)` in src/mint_python/ and tests/). Tag v0.4.0a5 on the bump commit.

ExecutionPolicy.worker-owns: Only module source files and module-local tests inside the approved write scope.
